api/Vulthil.Extensions.Hosting.IRestarta.title: Interface IRestartableHostedService | Vulthil.SharedKernel
api/Vulthil.Extensions.Hosting.IRestarta.summary: Interface IRestartableHostedService Namespace Vulthil.Extensions.Hosting Assembly Vulthil.Extensions.Hosting.dll Marks an IHostedService whose execution can be stopped and started again cleanly, so infrastructure may pause it for the duration of an operation that must not run concurrently with it — for example a test harness resetting the database between tests, where a live database-polling relay would otherwise contend with the reset. Implement this only on services whose StartAsync(CancellationToken) / StopAsync(CancellationToken) are idempotent across repeated cycles. public interface IRestartableHostedService : IHostedService Inherited Members IHostedService.StartAsync(CancellationToken) IHostedService.StopAsync(CancellationToken) Extension Methods ResultExtensions.ToResult<T>(T?, Error) Remarks Prefer implementing IHostedService directly over inheriting BackgroundService: the host observes only the execute task created by a BackgroundService's first start, and it treats a canceled execute task as a fault that stops the host whenever the application is not already shutting down. On .NET 10 that task is scheduled with a Task.Run gated on the stopping token, so a stop that races service startup can cancel the task before ExecuteAsync has run at all — no graceful handling inside ExecuteAsync can prevent the resulting host shutdown.

api/Vulthil.Extensions.Testing.Polling.h.summary: Class Polling Namespace Vulthil.Extensions.Testing Assembly Vulthil.Extensions.Testing.dll Provides polling utilities for waiting on asynchronous conditions during tests. public static class Polling Inheritance object Polling Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Fields Timeout Represents an error indicating that a polling operation has timed out. public static readonly Error Timeout Field Value Error Remarks Use this error to signal that a polling process did not complete within the allotted time. This error can be used to distinguish timeout conditions from other types of failures when handling polling results. Methods WaitAsync(TimeSpan, Func<CancellationToken, Task<Result>>, CancellationToken) Polls the provided function at one-second intervals until it returns a successful result or the timeout expires. The combined polling/timeout cancellation token is forwarded to the function so it can short-circuit work in progress. public static Task<PollingResult> WaitAsync(TimeSpan timeout, Func<CancellationToken, Task<Result>> func, CancellationToken cancellationToken) Parameters timeout TimeSpan The maximum duration to poll. func Func<CancellationToken, Task<Result>> The function to invoke each tick. Receives the shared cancellation token. cancellationToken CancellationToken A token to observe for cancellation. Linked internally with the polling timeout. api/Vulthil.Extensions.Testing.PollingEr.summary: Class PollingError Namespace Vulthil.Extensions.Testing Assembly Vulthil.Extensions.Testing.dll Represents a polling timeout error that aggregates all individual errors collected from each failed polling attempt. public sealed record PollingError : Error, IEquatable<Error>, IEquatable<PollingError> Inheritance object Error PollingError Implements IEquatable<Error> IEquatable<PollingError> Inherited Members Error.None Error.NullValue Error.Code Error.Description Error.Type Error.Failure(string, string) Error.NotFound(string, string) Error.Problem(string, string) Error.Validation(string, string) Error.Conflict(string, string) object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Extension Methods ResultExtensions.ToResult<T>(T?, Error) Remarks When a WaitAsync<T>(TimeSpan, Func<Task<Result<T>>>, TimeSpan, CancellationToken) call times out, the returned PollingResult<T> provides the PollingError via PollingError: var result = await Polling.WaitAsync(timeout, myFunc); if (result.IsFailure && result.PollingError is { } pollingError) { foreach (var error in pollingError.Errors) { // inspect each attempt's error } } Constructors PollingError(Error[]) Initializes a new instance of the PollingError record. public PollingError(Error[] errors) Parameters errors Error[] The individual errors from each polling attempt. Properties Errors Gets the individual errors collected during polling. Each element represents the error from a single failed attempt. public IReadOnlyList<Error> Errors { get; } Property Value IReadOnlyList<Error> Methods FromErrors(IEnumerable<Error>) Creates a PollingError from a collection of errors. public static PollingError FromErrors(IEnumerable<Error> errors) Parameters errors IEnumerable<Error> The errors collected during polling. Returns PollingError A PollingError containing all provided errors.

api/Vulthil.Messaging.Abstractions.Consu.title: Interface IConsumeFilter<TMessage> | Vulthil.SharedKernel
api/Vulthil.Messaging.Abstractions.Consu.summary: Interface IConsumeFilter<TMessage> Namespace Vulthil.Messaging.Abstractions.Consumers Assembly Vulthil.Messaging.Abstractions.dll A filter in the consume pipeline. Filters wrap the consumer invocation, allowing cross-cutting concerns (logging, validation, scoped resource management, telemetry, etc.) to be composed without modifying transport or consumer code. public interface IConsumeFilter<TMessage> where TMessage : notnull Type Parameters TMessage The message type the filter applies to. Extension Methods ResultExtensions.ToResult<T>(T?, Error) Remarks Filters are composed in registration order: the first registered filter is the outermost. A filter may short-circuit the pipeline by not invoking the next delegate, e.g. to reject a message based on a validation rule. Filters are resolved per delivery from the same scope as the consumer, so they may depend on scoped services (e.g. DbContext, scoped ILogger<T>). Methods ConsumeAsync(IMessageContext<TMessage>, ConsumeDelegate<TMessage>) Processes the message, optionally invoking next to continue the pipeline. Task ConsumeAsync(IMessageContext<TMessage> context, ConsumeDelegate<TMessage> next) Parameters context IMessageContext<TMessage> The message context. next ConsumeDelegate<TMessage> The next stage of the pipeline. The terminal stage invokes the consumer. Returns Task A task representing the asynchronous operation.

api/Vulthil.Messaging.Abstractions.Consu.title: Interface IMessageContext | Vulthil.SharedKernel
api/Vulthil.Messaging.Abstractions.Consu.summary: Interface IMessageContext Namespace Vulthil.Messaging.Abstractions.Consumers Assembly Vulthil.Messaging.Abstractions.dll Provides transport-level metadata for a received message. public interface IMessageContext Extension Methods ResultExtensions.ToResult<T>(T?, Error) Properties CancellationToken Gets the cancellation token associated with the current delivery. Combines the transport's stop signal with the consumer scope, allowing handlers to observe both. CancellationToken CancellationToken { get; } Property Value CancellationToken ConversationId Gets the conversation identifier that groups related messages across services, or null if not set. string? ConversationId { get; } Property Value string CorrelationId Gets the correlation identifier linking this message to a business transaction, or null if not set. string? CorrelationId { get; } Property Value string DestinationAddress Gets the intended destination address for the message, or null if not set. Uri? DestinationAddress { get; } Property Value Uri ExpirationTime Gets the UTC timestamp after which the message should be discarded, or null if it does not expire. DateTimeOffset? ExpirationTime { get; } Property Value DateTimeOffset? FaultAddress Gets the address where fault notifications should be sent, or null to use the default. Uri? FaultAddress { get; } Property Value Uri Headers Gets the transport headers associated with the message, containing custom metadata. IReadOnlyDictionary<string, object?> Headers { get; } Property Value IReadOnlyDictionary<string, object> Remarks Values are normalized when the context is built, so every consume path (envelope, bare AMQP, outbox relay) surfaces the same CLR primitives: strings arrive as string, booleans as bool, and numbers as int, long, or double — the narrowest of the three that represents the value. Header values published as non-primitive objects have no CLR type on the wire and surface in their JSON form: as a JsonElement when published as an object or array, or as a string for types JSON represents as strings (e.g. Guid, DateTimeOffset). InitiatorId Gets the identifier of the message that initiated this chain, or null if not set. string? InitiatorId { get; } Property Value string MessageId Gets the unique message identifier assigned by the transport, or null if not set. string? api/Vulthil.Messaging.DependencyInjectio.title: Class DependencyInjection | Vulthil.SharedKernel
api/Vulthil.Messaging.DependencyInjectio.summary: Class DependencyInjection Namespace Vulthil.Messaging Assembly Vulthil.Messaging.dll Provides extension methods for registering messaging services. public static class DependencyInjection Inheritance object DependencyInjection Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Methods AddMessaging(IHostApplicationBuilder, Action<IMessagingConfigurator>) Registers messaging services, queues, and consumers with the host application builder. public static IServiceCollection AddMessaging(this IHostApplicationBuilder builder, Action<IMessagingConfigurator> messagingConfiguratorAction) Parameters builder IHostApplicationBuilder The host application builder. messagingConfiguratorAction Action<IMessagingConfigurator> An action to configure messaging through IMessagingConfigurator. Returns IServiceCollection The service collection for chaining. Remarks Queue definitions and message configurations under Messaging:Queues: and Messaging:Messages: are loaded from IConfiguration before messagingConfiguratorAction runs. Code registrations performed inside the configurator action merge onto the loaded values and take precedence. Built-in consume filters (see ConsumeFilterOptions) are registered before the configurator action runs, so user-registered filters compose INSIDE the defaults. A transport must be configured — e.g. UseRabbitMq(...) inside the configurator, or registered separately (as ReplaceTransportWithTestHarness does) — otherwise the host fails to start with a clear error.

The filter is applied to every delivery whose message type matches the filter's IConsumeFilter<TMessage> interface. Multiple filters for the same message type are composed in registration order (first registered is outermost). IMessagingConfigurator AddConsumeFilter<TFilter>() where TFilter : class Returns IMessagingConfigurator The current configurator instance for chaining. Type Parameters TFilter The filter implementation. Must implement at least one IConsumeFilter<TMessage>. AddOpenConsumeFilter(Type) Registers an open-generic consume filter that applies to every message type. Use this for cross-cutting filters that do not depend on the typed payload (logging, telemetry, etc.). IMessagingConfigurator AddOpenConsumeFilter(Type openFilterType) Parameters openFilterType Type An open generic type (e.g. typeof(LoggingFilter<>)) implementing IConsumeFilter<>. Returns IMessagingConfigurator The current configurator instance for chaining. AddPublishFilter<TFilter>() Registers a publish/send filter applied to every outgoing publish and send. Multiple filters are composed in registration order (first registered is outermost). A filter may short-circuit the pipeline by not invoking next — for example a transactional outbox that captures the message into the database instead of sending it immediately. The filter is resolved from the caller's scope, so it may depend on scoped services. IMessagingConfigurator AddPublishFilter<TFilter>() where TFilter : class, IPublishFilter Returns IMessagingConfigurator The current configurator instance for chaining. Type Parameters TFilter The filter implementation, which must implement IPublishFilter. This allows you to specify exchange and routing configurations for outgoing messages of that type. Returns IMessagingConfigurator The current configurator instance for chaining. Type Parameters TMessage The type of the message to publish. Returns IMessagingConfigurator The current configurator instance for chaining. ConfigureQueue(string, Action<IQueueConfigurator>) Adds a named queue with consumer and routing configuration. Returns IMessagingConfigurator The current configurator instance for chaining. UsePartitioner<TMessage>(int) Serializes processing of TMessage deliveries by their CorrelationId — shorthand for the overload taking an explicit key selector with ctx => ctx.CorrelationId. Deliveries with no correlation id bypass the partitioner. IMessagingConfigurator UsePartitioner<TMessage>(int partitionCount) where TMessage : notnull Parameters partitionCount int The number of partitions (lanes) to distribute keys across. Returns IMessagingConfigurator The current configurator instance for chaining. Type Parameters TMessage The message type to partition. UsePartitioner<TMessage>(int, Func<IMessageContext<TMessage>, string?>) Serializes processing of TMessage deliveries that share a partition key, so messages correlated to the same aggregate are handled one at a time and in order, while messages with different keys process concurrently. Effective only when the consuming queue allows concurrency (ConcurrencyLimit > 1); at a concurrency of one, processing is already serial. IMessagingConfigurator UsePartitioner<TMessage>(int partitionCount, Func<IMessageContext<TMessage>, string?> keySelector) where TMessage : notnull Parameters partitionCount int The number of partitions (lanes) to distribute keys across. keySelector Func<IMessageContext<TMessage>, string> Selects the partition key from a delivery (e.g. ctx => ctx.CorrelationId). A delivery whose key is null or empty bypasses the partitioner. Returns IMessagingConfigurator The current configurator instance for chaining. Type Parameters TMessage The message type to partition. UsePartitioner<TMessage>(Partitioner) Serializes processing of TMessage deliveries by their CorrelationId using a caller-supplied Partitioner — shorthand for the overload taking an explicit key selector with ctx => ctx.CorrelationId. Share one Partitioner across several message types to serialize messages correlated to the same id regardless of their type (e.g. a saga). Deliveries with no correlation id bypass the partitioner. IMessagingConfigurator UsePartitioner<TMessage>(Partitioner partitioner) where TMessage : notnull Parameters partitioner Partitioner The partitioner whose lanes serialize same-key processing. Returns IMessagingConfigurator The current configurator instance for chaining. Type Parameters TMessage The message type to partition. UsePartitioner<TMessage>(Partitioner, Func<IMessageContext<TMessage>, string?>) Serializes processing of TMessage deliveries that share a partition key using a caller-supplied Partitioner. Share one Partitioner across several message types to serialize messages correlated to the same key regardless of their type (e.g. a saga). IMessagingConfigurator UsePartitioner<TMessage>(Partitioner partitioner, Func<IMessageContext<TMessage>, string?> keySelector) where TMessage : notnull Parameters partitioner Partitioner The partitioner whose lanes serialize same-key processing. keySelector Func<IMessageContext<TMessage>, string> Selects the partition key from a delivery (e.g. ctx => ctx.CorrelationId). A delivery whose key is null or empty bypasses the partitioner. Returns IMessagingConfigurator The current configurator instance for chaining. Type Parameters TMessage The message type to partition.

api/Vulthil.Messaging.ITransport.html.summary: Interface ITransport Namespace Vulthil.Messaging Assembly Vulthil.Messaging.dll Represents the message transport responsible for starting consumer connections. public interface ITransport Extension Methods ResultExtensions.ToResult<T>(T?, Error) Methods StartAsync(CancellationToken) Starts the transport, establishing connections and beginning message consumption. Task StartAsync(CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters cancellationToken CancellationToken A token to observe for cancellation. Returns Task A task representing the asynchronous startup operation. WaitUntilReadyAsync(CancellationToken) Completes once the transport has finished its startup pass — for a broker transport, once exchanges, queues, and subscriber bindings are declared, so a message published afterwards is routed to the subscriber queues. Background publishers (such as the outbox relay) should await this before their first publish to avoid losing messages sent before subscriber topology exists. The default implementation is ready immediately. Task WaitUntilReadyAsync(CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters cancellationToken CancellationToken A token to observe for cancellation. Returns Task A task that completes when the transport is ready to route published messages.

api/Vulthil.Messaging.Inbox.IIdempotency.title: Interface IIdempotencyStore | Vulthil.SharedKernel
api/Vulthil.Messaging.Inbox.IIdempotency.summary: Interface IIdempotencyStore Namespace Vulthil.Messaging.Inbox Assembly Vulthil.Messaging.Inbox.dll A persistence-agnostic store that records which messages have already been processed, enabling exactly-once consumer processing on a transactional store (effectively-once otherwise) on top of at-least-once delivery. public interface IIdempotencyStore Extension Methods ResultExtensions.ToResult<T>(T?, Error) Remarks The store owns the whole idempotent unit of work: it decides whether the delivery is a duplicate, runs the consumer, and records the idempotency marker — atomically with the consumer's business writes on a relational provider, or best-effort on a store without cross-partition transactions. Owning the unit lets a relational implementation run it inside an EF Core execution strategy, so it works whether or not a retrying execution strategy is configured. The reference relational implementation lives in Vulthil.Messaging.Inbox.Relational. Methods ProcessAsync(string, IMessageContext, Func<CancellationToken, Task>, CancellationToken) Processes the current delivery exactly once for the given idempotency key. If the key has not been seen, process is invoked and the idempotency marker is recorded — atomically with the consumer's business writes on a relational provider. If the key has already been recorded, process is skipped. Task<bool> ProcessAsync(string idempotencyKey, IMessageContext context, Func<CancellationToken, Task> process, CancellationToken cancellationToken) Parameters idempotencyKey string The idempotency key for the current delivery. context IMessageContext The message context for the current delivery. process Func<CancellationToken, Task> The consumer invocation to run when the delivery is not a duplicate. cancellationToken CancellationToken A token to observe for cancellation. Returns Task<bool> true if process ran and the marker was recorded; false if the delivery was a duplicate and was skipped.

GetKey(IMessageContext<in TMessage> context) Parameters context IMessageContext<TMessage> The message context for the current delivery. Returns string The idempotency key, or null or an empty string to use the message id.

api/Vulthil.Messaging.Inbox.IInboxRetent.summary: Interface IInboxRetentionStore Namespace Vulthil.Messaging.Inbox Assembly Vulthil.Messaging.Inbox.dll An IIdempotencyStore that can purge old idempotency markers for the inbox retention sweep. The EF Core idempotency stores implement this; the retention background service skips the sweep when the registered store does not. public interface IInboxRetentionStore Extension Methods ResultExtensions.ToResult<T>(T?, Error) Methods DeleteProcessedAsync(DateTimeOffset, int, CancellationToken) Deletes up to batchSize idempotency markers that were processed before olderThanUtc. Returns Task<int> The number of markers deleted.

api/Vulthil.Messaging.Inbox.InboxConfigu.summary: Class InboxConfiguratorExtensions Namespace Vulthil.Messaging.Inbox Assembly Vulthil.Messaging.Inbox.dll Configurator extensions that opt a message type into idempotent (inbox) processing. public static class InboxConfiguratorExtensions Inheritance object InboxConfiguratorExtensions Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks Opt-in is per message type: the idempotency guard is applied to every consumer of TMessage. A registered IIdempotencyStore is required at consume time — reference Vulthil.Messaging.Inbox.Relational (or supply your own store) to provide one. Methods AddIdempotentInbox<TMessage>(IMessagingConfigurator, Func<IMessageContext<TMessage>, string?>?) Enables idempotent processing for deliveries of TMessage. The guard skips messages whose idempotency key has already been processed and otherwise runs the consumer inside an IIdempotencyStore transaction so the marker and the consumer's writes commit atomically. public static IMessagingConfigurator AddIdempotentInbox<TMessage>(this IMessagingConfigurator configurator, Func<IMessageContext<TMessage>, string?>? keySelector = null) where TMessage : notnull Parameters configurator IMessagingConfigurator The messaging configurator. keySelector Func<IMessageContext<TMessage>, string> An optional selector for the idempotency key. When omitted (or when it returns null), the delivery's MessageId is used. Returns IMessagingConfigurator The same configurator, for chaining. Type Parameters TMessage The message type to guard.

api/Vulthil.Messaging.Inbox.InboxOptions.summary: Class InboxOptions Namespace Vulthil.Messaging.Inbox Assembly Vulthil.Messaging.Inbox.dll Configuration options for the idempotent-receiver (inbox) consume filter. public sealed class InboxOptions Inheritance object InboxOptions Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Extension Methods ResultExtensions.ToResult<T>(T?, Error) Properties EnableMetrics Gets or sets a value indicating whether inbox metrics (the vulthil.inbox.* counters on the MeterName meter) are auto-registered with OpenTelemetry. Default: true. public bool EnableMetrics { get; set; } Property Value bool RejectMessagesWithoutKey Gets or sets a value indicating whether a delivery with no resolvable idempotency key is rejected. api/Vulthil.Messaging.Inbox.InboxRetenti.title: Class InboxRetentionOptions | Vulthil.SharedKernel
api/Vulthil.Messaging.Inbox.InboxRetenti.summary: Class InboxRetentionOptions Namespace Vulthil.Messaging.Inbox Assembly Vulthil.Messaging.Inbox.dll Configuration for the inbox retention sweep, which periodically deletes idempotency markers once they are older than RetentionPeriod so the inbox table does not grow unbounded. public sealed class InboxRetentionOptions Inheritance object InboxRetentionOptions Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Extension Methods ResultExtensions.ToResult<T>(T?, Error) Properties BatchSize Gets or sets the maximum number of markers deleted per batch within a sweep; a sweep keeps deleting batches until fewer than this many markers remain. Default is 1000. [Range(1, 2147483647)] public int BatchSize { get; set; } Property Value int Enabled Gets or sets a value indicating whether the retention sweep runs. Default is false. public bool Enabled { get; set; } Property Value bool RetentionPeriod Gets or sets how long a marker is kept before it becomes eligible for deletion. Choose a value comfortably longer than the broker's maximum redelivery delay so a marker is never removed while a duplicate could still arrive. Default is 7 days. public TimeSpan RetentionPeriod { get; set; } Property Value TimeSpan SweepInterval Gets or sets the delay between retention sweeps. Default is 1 hour. public TimeSpan SweepInterval { get; set; } Property Value TimeSpan

api/Vulthil.Messaging.Outbox.Transaction.title: Class TransactionalOutboxExtensions | Vulthil.SharedKernel
api/Vulthil.Messaging.Outbox.Transaction.summary: Class TransactionalOutboxExtensions Namespace Vulthil.Messaging.Outbox Assembly Vulthil.Messaging.Outbox.dll Messaging-configurator extensions that enable the transactional bus-publish outbox. public static class TransactionalOutboxExtensions Inheritance object TransactionalOutboxExtensions Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Methods AddTransactionalConsumer<TMessage>(IMessagingConfigurator) Runs the consumer of TMessage inside a database transaction, so its database writes and any messages it publishes commit atomically and are captured by the transactional outbox — forcing the transaction around a consumer that does not otherwise open one (e.g. one without the inbox). If the inbox is also enabled for the type it opens the transaction first and this joins it. Requires a relational provider. public static IMessagingConfigurator AddTransactionalConsumer<TMessage>(this IMessagingConfigurator configurator) where TMessage : notnull Parameters configurator IMessagingConfigurator The messaging configurator. Returns IMessagingConfigurator The same configurator, for chaining. Type Parameters TMessage The consumed message type. AddTransactionalOutbox(IMessagingConfigurator) Captures publishes and sends issued while a database transaction is active into the shared outbox table and relays them to the broker after the transaction commits. Requires the shared outbox engine to be enabled on the application's DbContext (EnableOutboxProcessing), which registers the IOutboxStore the capture uses. public static IMessagingConfigurator AddTransactionalOutbox(this IMessagingConfigurator configurator) Parameters configurator IMessagingConfigurator The messaging configurator. Returns IMessagingConfigurator The same configurator, for chaining.

The transport resolves the concrete selector via the registered message type. public PartitionSpec(Partitioner Partitioner, Delegate KeySelector) Parameters Partitioner Partitioner KeySelector Delegate Properties KeySelector public Delegate KeySelector { get; init; } Property Value Delegate Partitioner public Partitioner Partitioner { get; init; } Property Value Partitioner

api/Vulthil.Messaging.Partitioner.html.title: Class Partitioner | Vulthil.SharedKernel
api/Vulthil.Messaging.Partitioner.html.summary: Class Partitioner Namespace Vulthil.Messaging Assembly Vulthil.Messaging.dll Distributes work across a fixed number of ordered partitions ("lanes"), selected by a hash of a partition key. Work submitted for the same key runs strictly sequentially and in submission order; work for keys that hash to different lanes runs concurrently. public sealed class Partitioner Inheritance object Partitioner Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Extension Methods ResultExtensions.ToResult<T>(T?, Error) Remarks Used on the consume side to give per-aggregate ordering — messages correlated to the same aggregate are processed one at a time and in order, while unrelated messages still process in parallel. A single instance can be shared across multiple message types so that messages correlated to the same key are serialized together regardless of their type. The hash is in-process only, so the lane assignment for a given key need not be stable across processes. Constructors Partitioner(int) Initializes a new Partitioner with the specified number of partitions. public Partitioner(int partitionCount) Parameters partitionCount int The number of lanes. A larger count lets more distinct keys make progress concurrently; the count affects only fan-out, never correctness. Exceptions ArgumentOutOfRangeException Thrown when partitionCount is not positive. Properties PartitionCount Gets the number of partitions (lanes) this partitioner distributes work across. public int PartitionCount { get; } Property Value int Methods RunSequentialAsync(string, Func<Task>) Queues work on the lane selected by key, running it only after all work already queued on that lane has completed. Submissions for the same key therefore run sequentially and in submission order; a failed unit of work does not block subsequent work on the lane. public Task RunSequentialAsync(string key, Func<Task> work) Parameters key string The partition key. work Func<Task> The work to run on the key's lane. Returns Task A task that completes when work completes (and faults if it faults).

api/Vulthil.Messaging.Queues.IQueueConfi.title: Interface IQueueConfigurator | Vulthil.SharedKernel
api/Vulthil.Messaging.Queues.IQueueConfi.summary: Interface IQueueConfigurator Namespace Vulthil.Messaging.Queues Assembly Vulthil.Messaging.dll Configures a queue's consumers, retry policies, and dead letter settings. public interface IQueueConfigurator Extension Methods ResultExtensions.ToResult<T>(T?, Error) Methods AddConsumer<TConsumer>(Action<IConsumerConfigurator<TConsumer>>?) Registers a one-way consumer on this queue with optional per-consumer configuration. If the consumer's TMessage is concrete, the queue is auto-subscribed to it at build time; for polymorphic consumers (e.g. IConsumer<IOrderEvent>) the caller must explicitly Subscribe<TMessage>(string?) the concrete implementers. IQueueConfigurator AddConsumer<TConsumer>(Action<IConsumerConfigurator<TConsumer>>? configure = null) where TConsumer : class, IConsumer Parameters configure Action<IConsumerConfigurator<TConsumer>> Returns IQueueConfigurator Type Parameters TConsumer AddRequestConsumer<TConsumer>(Action<IRequestConfigurator<TConsumer>>?) Registers a request/reply consumer on this queue with optional per-consumer configuration. IQueueConfigurator AddRequestConsumer<TConsumer>(Action<IRequestConfigurator<TConsumer>>? configure = null) where TConsumer : class, IRequestConsumer Parameters configure Action<IRequestConfigurator<TConsumer>> Returns IQueueConfigurator Type Parameters TConsumer ConfigureQueue(Action<QueueDefinition>) Applies additional configuration to the underlying QueueDefinition. IQueueConfigurator ConfigureQueue(Action<QueueDefinition> configureAction) Parameters configureAction Action<QueueDefinition> Returns IQueueConfigurator SubscribeAll<TInterface>(Assembly, string?) Discovers every concrete (non-abstract, non-interface) type in assembly that is assignable to TInterface, and calls Subscribe<TMessage>(string?) for each. Pair with a polymorphic AddConsumer<TConsumer>() (where TConsumer : IConsumer<TInterface>) to dispatch all implementers through one consumer. IQueueConfigurator SubscribeAll<TInterface>(Assembly assembly, string? routingKey = null) where TInterface : class Parameters assembly Assembly The assembly to scan for concrete implementers. routingKey string The binding pattern applied to every discovered implementer's exchange. null = broker default. Returns IQueueConfigurator Type Parameters TInterface The polymorphic dispatch type — typically an interface or abstract base class. Subscribe<TMessage>(string?) Subscribes this queue to receive deliveries of the concrete message type TMessage. At topology setup time, the queue is bound to TMessage's exchange with the supplied routingKey pattern (the broker uses this to filter; the worker does not re-filter). Abstract types and interfaces are rejected — those have no exchange; use SubscribeAll<TInterface>(Assembly, string?) or call Subscribe<TMessage>(string?) for each concrete implementer instead. IQueueConfigurator Subscribe<TMessage>(string? routingKey = null) where TMessage : class Parameters routingKey string The binding pattern. When null, the broker receives an empty string — fanout/headers exchanges ignore it; direct exchanges only deliver messages with an empty published routing key; topic exchanges match no patterns. For non-empty needs, supply a specific pattern (e.g. "order.created" for direct, "order.*" for topic). Returns IQueueConfigurator Type Parameters TMessage A concrete (non-abstract, non-interface) message type. UseDeadLetterQueue(string?, string?) Enables a dead letter queue for this queue. IQueueConfigurator UseDeadLetterQueue(string? queueName = null, string? exchangeName = null) Parameters queueName string Optional dead letter queue name. exchangeName string Optional dead letter exchange name. Returns IQueueConfigurator UseRetry(Action<RetryPolicyConfigurator>) Configures the default retry policy for all consumers on this queue. IQueueConfigurator UseRetry(Action<RetryPolicyConfigurator> configure) Parameters configure Action<RetryPolicyConfigurator> Returns IQueueConfigurator UseSingleActiveConsumer() Declares the queue with RabbitMQ's single active consumer feature: only one consumer is active at a time while additional consumers stand by and take over on failure. This preserves per-queue order across load-balanced consumer instances — extending the in-process partitioner's ordering guarantee to multiple instances — at the cost of throughput scale-out for the queue. Partitioned queues enable this automatically. IQueueConfigurator UseSingleActiveConsumer() Returns IQueueConfigurator

api/Vulthil.Messaging.Queues.QueueDefini.summary: Class QueueDefinition Namespace Vulthil.Messaging.Queues Assembly Vulthil.Messaging.dll Describes a queue including its consumer registrations, exchange bindings, and runtime settings. public sealed record QueueDefinition : IEquatable<QueueDefinition> Inheritance object QueueDefinition Implements IEquatable<QueueDefinition> Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Extension Methods ResultExtensions.ToResult<T>(T?, Error) Constructors QueueDefinition(string) Describes a queue including its consumer registrations, exchange bindings, and runtime settings. public QueueDefinition(string Name) Parameters Name string The initial name of the queue. Properties AutoDelete Gets or sets a value indicating whether this queue is automatically deleted when the last consumer disconnects. public bool AutoDelete { get; set; } Property Value bool ChannelCount Gets or sets the number of channels to open for this queue. Default is 1. public ushort ChannelCount { get; set; } Property Value ushort ConcurrencyLimit Gets or sets the maximum number of concurrent consumers per channel. Default is 1. public ushort ConcurrencyLimit { get; set; } Property Value ushort DeadLetter Gets or sets the dead letter configuration for this queue, or null if disabled. public DeadLetterDefinition? DeadLetter { get; set; } Property Value DeadLetterDefinition DefaultRetryPolicy Gets or sets the default retry policy applied to all consumers on this queue. public RetryPolicyDefinition? DefaultRetryPolicy { get; set; } Property Value RetryPolicyDefinition Durable Gets or sets a value indicating whether this queue is durable. Default is true. public bool Durable { get; set; } Property Value bool ExchangeArguments Gets additional arguments passed to the broker during exchange declaration. public IDictionary<string, object?> ExchangeArguments { get; } Property Value IDictionary<string, object> ExchangeAutoDelete Gets or sets a value indicating whether the exchange is automatically deleted when no queues are bound. public bool ExchangeAutoDelete { get; set; } Property Value bool ExchangeDurable Gets or sets a value indicating whether the exchange is durable. Default is true. public bool ExchangeDurable { get; set; } Property Value bool ExchangeType Gets or sets the exchange type for the queue's exchange binding. Default is Fanout. public MessagingExchangeType ExchangeType { get; set; } Property Value MessagingExchangeType Exclusive Gets or sets a value indicating whether this queue is exclusive to the declaring connection. public bool Exclusive { get; set; } Property Value bool IsQuorum Gets or sets a value indicating whether this queue uses quorum replication. Default is true. public bool IsQuorum { get; set; } Property Value bool Name Gets or sets the queue name. public string Name { get; set; } Property Value string PrefetchCount Gets or sets the prefetch count (number of unacknowledged messages). Default is 16. public ushort PrefetchCount { get; set; } Property Value ushort Registrations Gets the collection of consumer-to-message bindings configured for this queue. public IReadOnlyCollection<Registration> Registrations { get; } Property Value IReadOnlyCollection<Registration> RetryEnabled Gets a value indicating whether any retry policy is configured, either at the queue or consumer level. public bool RetryEnabled { get; } Property Value bool SingleActiveConsumer Gets or sets a value indicating whether the queue is declared with RabbitMQ's single active consumer feature, so that only one consumer processes deliveries at a time (additional consumers stand by and take over on failure). This preserves per-queue order across load-balanced consumer instances at the cost of throughput scale-out for the queue. Partitioned queues enable this automatically. Default is false. public bool SingleActiveConsumer { get; set; } Property Value bool Subscriptions Gets the exchange bindings configured for this queue — each Subscription becomes one exchange→queue binding declared at topology setup time. Populated by Subscribe<TMessage>(string?) and SubscribeAll<TInterface>(Assembly, string?), and auto-populated by Build for any consumer whose concrete TMessage isn't explicitly subscribed (the consumer's routing-key pattern is carried into the subscription). public IReadOnlyCollection<Subscription> Subscriptions { get; } Property Value IReadOnlyCollection<Subscription> Methods AddConsumer(Registration) Adds a consumer registration to this queue. Duplicate registrations (by value) are ignored. public void AddConsumer(Registration registration) Parameters registration Registration The consumer-to-message binding to add. AddSubscription(Subscription) Adds an exchange→queue binding to this queue. Duplicate subscriptions (by value) are ignored. public void AddSubscription(Subscription subscription) Parameters subscription Subscription The subscription describing the binding to add.

api/Vulthil.Messaging.Queues.RetryPolicy.summary: Class RetryPolicyConfigurator Namespace Vulthil.Messaging.Queues Assembly Vulthil.Messaging.dll Fluent builder for constructing a RetryPolicyDefinition. public sealed class RetryPolicyConfigurator Inheritance object RetryPolicyConfigurator Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Extension Methods ResultExtensions.ToResult<T>(T?, Error) Properties IgnoredExceptions Gets the exception types that should be excluded from retry attempts and immediately surfaced. public IReadOnlySet<Type> IgnoredExceptions { get; } Property Value IReadOnlySet<Type> Intervals Gets the configured delay intervals between successive retry attempts. public IReadOnlyList<TimeSpan> Intervals { get; } Property Value IReadOnlyList<TimeSpan> RetryLimit Gets the maximum number of retry attempts configured via one of the interval methods. public int RetryLimit { get; } Property Value int Methods Build() Builds the RetryPolicyDefinition from the current configuration. public RetryPolicyDefinition Build() Returns RetryPolicyDefinition The constructed retry policy definition. Exponential(int, TimeSpan, TimeSpan, double) Configures exponential back-off retry intervals. public void Exponential(int retryCount, TimeSpan initialInterval, TimeSpan maxInterval, double multiplier = 2) Parameters retryCount int The number of retries. initialInterval TimeSpan The delay for the first retry. maxInterval TimeSpan The maximum delay cap. multiplier double The multiplier applied to each successive interval. Default is 2.0. Ignore<TException>() Adds an exception type to the ignore list; matching exceptions will not trigger retries. public void Ignore<TException>() where TException : Exception Type Parameters TException The exception type to ignore. Immediate(int) Configures immediate retries with zero delay. public void Immediate(int retryCount) Parameters retryCount int The number of retries. InMemory() Configures retries to run in-memory: the consumer is re-invoked in-process while the delivery is held, instead of being re-delivered later through the retry queue. This preserves message order, so it is the behavior used automatically for partitioned queues. public void InMemory() SetIntervals(params TimeSpan[]) Configures explicit retry intervals. public void SetIntervals(params TimeSpan[] intervals) Parameters intervals TimeSpan[] The delay between each retry attempt. UseJitter(double) Adds random jitter to retry intervals. public void UseJitter(double factor) Parameters factor double The jitter factor between 0.0 and 1.0. Exceptions ArgumentOutOfRangeException Thrown when factor is outside the valid range.

api/Vulthil.Messaging.TestHarness.ITestH.title: Interface ITestHarness | Vulthil.SharedKernel
api/Vulthil.Messaging.TestHarness.ITestH.summary: Interface ITestHarness Namespace Vulthil.Messaging.TestHarness Assembly Vulthil.Messaging.TestHarness.dll In-memory messaging test harness. Captures every message produced and consumed through the in-memory transport so tests can assert on messaging behaviour with no broker, and lets a test stand in for an external service by handling published messages or responding to requests. public interface ITestHarness Extension Methods ResultExtensions.ToResult<T>(T?, Error) Remarks The harness dispatches synchronously: by the time a publish, send, or request call completes, every consumer (and registered Handle<TMessage>(Func<IMessageContext<TMessage>, Task>)/Respond<TRequest, TResponse>(Func<IMessageContext<TRequest>, TResponse>) stub) it triggered has run, so assertions need no polling. A one-way consumer that throws is retried per its configured policy and, once the attempts are exhausted, a Fault<T> is published — the publish or send itself still completes — mirroring the broker transport; a request consumer's exception is surfaced as a failed request result. Methods Clear() Clears all captured published, sent, consumed, and requested messages. Registered stubs are retained. void Clear() Consumed<TMessage>() Gets the messages delivered to a registered consumer that are assignable to TMessage, in order. A message handled by several consumers is captured once per consumer. IReadOnlyList<CapturedMessage<TMessage>> Consumed<TMessage>() where TMessage : notnull Returns IReadOnlyList<CapturedMessage<TMessage>> Type Parameters TMessage The message type to filter by. Handle<TMessage>(Func<IMessageContext<TMessage>, Task>) Registers a stub that runs whenever a TMessage is published or sent, in addition to any registered consumers. Use it to stand in for a downstream service — the stub can publish or send follow-up messages through its context. void Handle<TMessage>(Func<IMessageContext<TMessage>, Task> handler) where TMessage : notnull Parameters handler Func<IMessageContext<TMessage>, Task> The stub invoked with the delivered message context. Type Parameters TMessage The message type to react to. Published<TMessage>() Gets the messages published via IPublisher.PublishAsync that are assignable to TMessage, in order. IReadOnlyList<CapturedMessage<TMessage>> Published<TMessage>() where TMessage : notnull Returns IReadOnlyList<CapturedMessage<TMessage>> Type Parameters TMessage The message type to filter by. Requested<TMessage>() Gets the requests issued via IRequester.RequestAsync that are assignable to TMessage, in order. IReadOnlyList<CapturedMessage<TMessage>> Requested<TMessage>() where TMessage : notnull Returns IReadOnlyList<CapturedMessage<TMessage>> Type Parameters TMessage The request type to filter by. Respond<TRequest, TResponse>(Func<IMessageContext<TRequest>, TResponse>) Registers a stub that answers requests of type TRequest with a TResponse, standing in for an external request consumer. A registered responder takes precedence over a real request consumer for the same request type. void Respond<TRequest, TResponse>(Func<IMessageContext<TRequest>, TResponse> responder) where TRequest : notnull where TResponse : notnull Parameters responder Func<IMessageContext<TRequest>, TResponse> The stub invoked with the request context to produce the response. Type Parameters TRequest The request type to answer. TResponse The response type to return. Sent<TMessage>() Gets the messages sent via ISendEndpoint.SendAsync that are assignable to TMessage, in order. IReadOnlyList<CapturedMessage<TMessage>> Sent<TMessage>() where TMessage : notnull Returns IReadOnlyList<CapturedMessage<TMessage>> Type Parameters TMessage The message type to filter by.

api/Vulthil.Messaging.TestHarness.TestHa.title: Class TestHarnessExtensions | Vulthil.SharedKernel
api/Vulthil.Messaging.TestHarness.TestHa.summary: Class TestHarnessExtensions Namespace Vulthil.Messaging.TestHarness Assembly Vulthil.Messaging.TestHarness.dll Registers the in-memory ITestHarness transport. The harness reuses the messaging configuration (queues, consumers, message settings) that AddMessaging registered, so it mirrors the real topology with no broker. public static class TestHarnessExtensions Inheritance object TestHarnessExtensions Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Methods ReplaceTransportWithTestHarness(IServiceCollection) Replaces an already-registered transport (e.g. RabbitMQ) with the in-memory test harness, leaving the rest of the application's composition untouched. Call this from a test host's service-configuration hook (for example a WebApplicationFactory) so production code is not modified for tests. The orphaned broker registrations remain but are never resolved, so no broker connection is attempted. public static IServiceCollection ReplaceTransportWithTestHarness(this IServiceCollection services) Parameters services IServiceCollection The service collection whose transport registrations are replaced. Returns IServiceCollection The same service collection, for chaining. UseTestHarness(IMessagingConfigurator) Configures the messaging infrastructure to use the in-memory test harness as its transport. Call this in place of a broker transport (e.g. UseRabbitMq) when composing messaging for a test. public static IMessagingConfigurator UseTestHarness(this IMessagingConfigurator configurator) Parameters configurator IMessagingConfigurator The messaging configurator. Returns IMessagingConfigurator The same configurator, for chaining.

api/Vulthil.Messaging.Transport.IPublish.title: Interface IPublishFilter | Vulthil.SharedKernel
api/Vulthil.Messaging.Transport.IPublish.summary: Interface IPublishFilter Namespace Vulthil.Messaging.Transport Assembly Vulthil.Messaging.dll A filter in the publish/send pipeline. Filters wrap the transport send, allowing cross-cutting concerns (transactional outbox capture, logging, validation, telemetry, …) to be composed without modifying transport or caller code. public interface IPublishFilter Extension Methods ResultExtensions.ToResult<T>(T?, Error) Remarks Filters are composed in registration order: the first registered filter is the outermost. A filter may short-circuit the pipeline by not invoking the next delegate. Filters are resolved from the caller's scope, so they may depend on scoped services (e.g. DbContext). The same filter sees both publishes and sends; use Kind to distinguish them. Methods PublishAsync(PublishFilterContext, PublishFilterDelegate) Processes the outgoing message, optionally invoking next to continue the pipeline. Task PublishAsync(PublishFilterContext context, PublishFilterDelegate next) Parameters context PublishFilterContext The publish filter context. next PublishFilterDelegate The next stage of the pipeline. The terminal stage performs the transport send. Returns Task A task representing the asynchronous operation.

api/Vulthil.Messaging.Transport.MessageC.title: Class MessageContext | Vulthil.SharedKernel
api/Vulthil.Messaging.Transport.MessageC.summary: Class MessageContext Namespace Vulthil.Messaging.Transport Assembly Vulthil.Messaging.dll Transport-agnostic IMessageContext implementation describing a delivered message and the transport services that back its PublishAsync<TMessage>(TMessage, Func<IPublishContext, ValueTask>?) and SendAsync<TMessage>(Uri, TMessage, Func<IPublishContext, ValueTask>?) helpers. A transport builds one per delivery (typically via CreateFromEnvelope<TMessage>(TMessage, MessageEnvelope, string, bool, int, string?, IPublisher?, ISendEndpointProvider?, CancellationToken)); a context with no live transport binding (e.g. a fault snapshot) throws when a publish or send is attempted. public record MessageContext : IMessageContext, IEquatable<MessageContext> Inheritance object MessageContext Implements IMessageContext IEquatable<MessageContext> Derived MessageContext<TMessage> Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Extension Methods ResultExtensions.ToResult<T>(T?, Error) Properties CancellationToken Gets the cancellation token associated with the current delivery. Combines the transport's stop signal with the consumer scope, allowing handlers to observe both. public CancellationToken CancellationToken { get; init; } Property Value CancellationToken ConversationId Gets the conversation identifier that groups related messages across services, or null if not set. public string? ConversationId { get; init; } Property Value string CorrelationId Gets the correlation identifier linking this message to a business transaction, or null if not set. public string? CorrelationId { get; init; } Property Value string DestinationAddress Gets the intended destination address for the message, or null if not set. public Uri? DestinationAddress { get; init; } Property Value Uri ExpirationTime Gets the UTC timestamp after which the message should be discarded, or null if it does not expire. public DateTimeOffset? ExpirationTime { get; init; } Property Value DateTimeOffset? FaultAddress Gets the address where fault notifications should be sent, or null to use the default. public Uri? FaultAddress { get; init; } Property Value Uri Headers Gets the transport headers associated with the message, containing custom metadata. public required IReadOnlyDictionary<string, object?> Headers { get; init; } Property Value IReadOnlyDictionary<string, object> Remarks Values are normalized when the context is built, so every consume path (envelope, bare AMQP, outbox relay) surfaces the same CLR primitives: strings arrive as string, booleans as bool, and numbers as int, long, or double — the narrowest of the three that represents the value. Header values published as non-primitive objects have no CLR type on the wire and surface in their JSON form: as a JsonElement when published as an object or array, or as a string for types JSON represents as strings (e.g. Guid, DateTimeOffset). InitiatorId Gets the identifier of the message that initiated this chain, or null if not set. public string? InitiatorId { get; init; } Property Value string MessageId Gets the unique message identifier assigned by the transport, or null if not set. public string? MessageId { get; init; } Property Value string Publisher Gets the publisher backing PublishAsync<TMessage>(TMessage, Func<IPublishContext, ValueTask>?), or null for a snapshot context not bound to a live transport. public IPublisher? Publisher { get; init; } Property Value IPublisher Redelivered Gets a value indicating whether the broker redelivered this message (e.g., after a consumer crash). public bool Redelivered { get; init; } Property Value bool RequestId Gets the request identifier used to correlate a reply back to the original request, or null if not set. public string? RequestId { get; init; } Property Value string ResponseAddress Gets the address where replies should be sent, or null if no reply is expected. public Uri? ResponseAddress { get; init; } Property Value Uri RetryCount Gets the number of times this message has been retried. A value of 0 indicates the first delivery attempt. public int RetryCount { get; init; } Property Value int RoutingKey Gets the routing key that was used by the transport to deliver this message. public required string RoutingKey { get; init; } Property Value string SendEndpointProvider Gets the send endpoint provider backing SendAsync<TMessage>(Uri, TMessage, Func<IPublishContext, ValueTask>?), or null for a snapshot context not bound to a live transport. public ISendEndpointProvider? SendEndpointProvider { get; init; } Property Value ISendEndpointProvider SentTime Gets the UTC timestamp when the message was originally sent, or null if not recorded. public DateTimeOffset? SentTime { get; init; } Property Value DateTimeOffset? SourceAddress Gets the address of the endpoint that sent the message, or null if unknown. public Uri? SourceAddress { get; init; } Property Value Uri Methods CreateFromEnvelope<TMessage>(TMessage, MessageEnvelope, string, bool, int, string?, IPublisher?, ISendEndpointProvider?, CancellationToken) Builds a live typed context from a parsed MessageEnvelope. Transport-level fields the envelope does not carry are supplied by the caller: the broker routingKey, the redelivered flag, the in-memory retryCount, and a replyToFallback used for the response address when the envelope omits one. Header values deserialized from the wire are normalized to CLR primitives (see Headers for the contract). public static MessageContext<TMessage> CreateFromEnvelope<TMessage>(TMessage message, MessageEnvelope envelope, string routingKey, bool redelivered, int retryCount, string? replyToFallback, IPublisher? publisher, ISendEndpointProvider? sendEndpointProvider, CancellationToken cancellationToken) Parameters message TMessage The deserialized message. envelope MessageEnvelope The parsed wire envelope supplying the message metadata. routingKey string The broker routing key the message arrived on. redelivered bool Whether the broker flagged this as a redelivery. retryCount int The current in-memory retry attempt. replyToFallback string A reply destination to use when the envelope carries no response address, or null. publisher IPublisher The publisher backing PublishAsync<TMessage>(TMessage, Func<IPublishContext, ValueTask>?), or null. sendEndpointProvider ISendEndpointProvider The provider backing SendAsync<TMessage>(Uri, TMessage, Func<IPublishContext, ValueTask>?), or null. cancellationToken CancellationToken The token observed while consuming the message. Returns MessageContext<TMessage> The constructed typed context. Type Parameters TMessage The deserialized message type. PublishAsync<TMessage>(TMessage, Func<IPublishContext, ValueTask>?) Publishes a message via the underlying transport, automatically propagating correlation metadata (CorrelationId, ConversationId, InitiatorId) from the incoming context to the outgoing message. The caller-supplied configure callback runs after propagation, so it can override any auto-set value. public Task PublishAsync<TMessage>(TMessage message, Func<IPublishContext, ValueTask>? configure = null) where TMessage : notnull Parameters message TMessage The message to publish. configure Func<IPublishContext, ValueTask> Optional callback for customizing the outgoing publish context. Returns Task A task representing the asynchronous publish operation. Type Parameters TMessage The type of message to publish. SendAsync<TMessage>(Uri, TMessage, Func<IPublishContext, ValueTask>?) Sends a message point-to-point to the specified destination, automatically propagating correlation metadata (CorrelationId, ConversationId, InitiatorId) from the incoming context to the outgoing message. The caller-supplied configure callback runs after propagation, so it can override any auto-set value. public Task SendAsync<TMessage>(Uri destinationAddress, TMessage message, Func<IPublishContext, ValueTask>? configure = null) where TMessage : notnull Parameters destinationAddress Uri The destination endpoint address (e.g. queue:order-commands). message TMessage The message to send. configure Func<IPublishContext, ValueTask> Optional callback for customizing the outgoing publish context. Returns Task A task representing the asynchronous send operation. Type Parameters TMessage The type of message to send.

api/Vulthil.Messaging.Transport.MessageE.title: Class MessageExecutionRegistry<THandler> | Vulthil.SharedKernel
api/Vulthil.Messaging.Transport.MessageE.summary: Class MessageExecutionRegistry<THandler> Namespace Vulthil.Messaging.Transport Assembly Vulthil.Messaging.dll Transport-agnostic registry that turns QueueDefinition registrations into per-message-type MessageExecutionPlan<THandler> instances. It keys plans by wire URN (and CLR full name for the bare-JSON receive path), fans a polymorphic registration out across every matching concrete subscription, dedupes handlers, rejects a second request consumer for the same message type on the same queue, and attaches the partition specification read from GetPartition(Type). A transport drives it by supplying an IMessageHandlerFactory<THandler> that builds its own delivery closures. public sealed class MessageExecutionRegistry<THandler> where THandler : notnull Type Parameters THandler The transport-specific handler type produced by the factory and stored in plans. Inheritance object MessageExecutionRegistry<THandler> Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Extension Methods ResultExtensions.ToResult<T>(T?, Error) Constructors MessageExecutionRegistry(IMessageConfigurationProvider, IMessageHandlerFactory<THandler>) Initializes a new instance of the MessageExecutionRegistry<THandler> class. public MessageExecutionRegistry(IMessageConfigurationProvider provider, IMessageHandlerFactory<THandler> handlerFactory) Parameters provider IMessageConfigurationProvider The resolved messaging configuration, used for URN and partition lookups. handlerFactory IMessageHandlerFactory<THandler> The transport factory that builds handlers for each registration. Properties Plans Gets the execution plans assembled so far, one per concrete message type, in no particular order. public IReadOnlyCollection<MessageExecutionPlan<THandler>> Plans { get; } Property Value IReadOnlyCollection<MessageExecutionPlan<THandler>> Methods GetPlan(string) Convenience lookup used by the bare-JSON receive path and tests: tries URN parsing first, then falls back to CLR full name. public MessageExecutionPlan<THandler>? GetPlan(string key) Parameters key string A wire URN or CLR full type name. Returns MessageExecutionPlan<THandler> The matching plan, or null. GetPlanByFullName(string) Resolves a plan from the CLR full type name (bare-JSON compatibility path). Returns null when no plan matches. public MessageExecutionPlan<THandler>? GetPlanByFullName(string fullName) Parameters fullName string The CLR full type name. Returns MessageExecutionPlan<THandler> The matching plan, or null. GetPlanByUrn(Uri) Resolves a plan from the wire URN (envelope path). Returns null when no plan matches. public MessageExecutionPlan<THandler>? GetPlanByUrn(Uri urn) Parameters urn Uri The wire URN as it appeared on the envelope. Returns MessageExecutionPlan<THandler> The matching plan, or null. IsQueuePartitioned(QueueDefinition) Indicates whether any concrete message type subscribed or consumed by queue is partitioned, read directly from GetPartition(Type). Because it does not depend on built plans it is valid both during topology setup (which precedes RegisterQueue(QueueDefinition)) and afterwards. Drives ordered single dispatch and the single-active-consumer queue argument. public bool IsQueuePartitioned(QueueDefinition queue) Parameters queue QueueDefinition The queue to inspect. Returns bool true when any concrete message type on the queue is partitioned; otherwise false. RegisterQueue(QueueDefinition) Assembles execution plans for every concrete message type consumed or subscribed by queue. Plans are keyed by URN within this registry instance, so registering several queues that consume the same message type accumulates all their handlers in one plan. A transport that delivers per queue must therefore build one registry per queue so a queue's deliveries dispatch only its own handlers (as the RabbitMQ transport does); a transport that dispatches each produced message exactly once can register every queue in a single instance (as the in-memory test harness does). A message type can have at most one request consumer per queue. public void RegisterQueue(QueueDefinition queue) Parameters queue QueueDefinition The queue whose registrations and subscriptions to register. Exceptions InvalidOperationException A second request consumer is registered for a message type that already has one on the same queue.

api/Vulthil.Messaging.Transport.html.title: Namespace Vulthil.Messaging.Transport | Vulthil.SharedKernel
api/Vulthil.Messaging.Transport.html.summary: Namespace Vulthil.Messaging.Transport Classes ConsumePipelineFactory Composes the registered IConsumeFilter<TMessage> instances into a single delegate. Transport authors call this in their consume path to wrap a terminal stage (the consumer invocation) with the configured middleware. HandlerEntry<THandler> Pairs a transport-specific dispatch handler with the consumer contract it implements. Returned by IMessageHandlerFactory<THandler> so MessageExecutionRegistry<THandler> can enforce request-consumer uniqueness without inspecting the opaque THandler value. MessageContext Transport-agnostic IMessageContext implementation describing a delivered message and the transport services that back its PublishAsync<TMessage>(TMessage, Func<IPublishContext, ValueTask>?) and SendAsync<TMessage>(Uri, TMessage, Func<IPublishContext, ValueTask>?) helpers. A transport builds one per delivery (typically via CreateFromEnvelope<TMessage>(TMessage, MessageEnvelope, string, bool, int, string?, IPublisher?, ISendEndpointProvider?, CancellationToken)); a context with no live transport binding (e.g. a fault snapshot) throws when a publish or send is attempted. MessageContext<TMessage> A strongly-typed MessageContext carrying the deserialized message payload. MessageEnvelope On-the-wire wrapper for messages carried by a Vulthil transport. The body of every produced message is a serialized MessageEnvelope; receivers fall back to bare JSON only for compatibility with non-Vulthil producers. MessageEnvelopeFactory Builds MessageEnvelope instances from the resolved publish state for a single outgoing message. Promotes the reserved metadata headers carried by a PublishContext to typed envelope fields and copies the remaining custom headers verbatim. MessageExecutionPlan<THandler> Transport-agnostic execution plan for a single concrete message type: the ordered set of handlers that run on every delivery, plus the partition specification when the type is partitioned for ordered consumption. Built and keyed by MessageExecutionRegistry<THandler>. MessageExecutionRegistry<THandler> Transport-agnostic registry that turns QueueDefinition registrations into per-message-type MessageExecutionPlan<THandler> instances. It keys plans by wire URN (and CLR full name for the bare-JSON receive path), fans a polymorphic registration out across every matching concrete subscription, dedupes handlers, rejects a second request consumer for the same message type on the same queue, and attaches the partition specification read from GetPartition(Type). A transport drives it by supplying an IMessageHandlerFactory<THandler> that builds its own delivery closures. PublishContext Mutable, write-through configuration captured for a single outgoing message. A transport creates one, passes it to the caller's configure callback as IPublishContext, then reads the resolved values to build its wire message. Metadata that maps to typed envelope fields is stored under reserved header keys and surfaced through the strongly-typed properties. PublishFilterContext The context flowing through the publish/send filter pipeline for a single outgoing message. A filter may inspect the message and its resolved metadata, adjust Context, short-circuit the pipeline by not invoking next (e.g. to capture the message into a transactional outbox), or let it proceed to the transport terminal. PublishFilteringServiceCollectionExtensions Registers the publish/send filtering facade in front of a transport's raw publish terminal. A transport registers its publisher and send-endpoint provider under ITransportPublisher / ITransportSendEndpointProvider and then calls AddPublishFiltering(IServiceCollection) so the public IPublisher / ISendEndpointProvider run every publish and send through the pipeline. PublishPipelineFactory Composes the registered IPublishFilter instances around a terminal delegate (the transport send). The filtering publisher/send-endpoint call this in their publish path to wrap the transport with the configured middleware. RequestContext A PublishContext for request/reply: adds the per-request timeout. A transport creates one, passes it to the caller's configure callback as IRequestContext, then reads Timeout to bound the wait for a response. RpcFault Wire payload for an RPC failure reply. Carried as the message of a MessageEnvelope whose messageType is UrnUri. Property names are fixed (camelCase) so the reply round-trips regardless of the configured JSON naming policy. Interfaces IMessageHandlerFactory<THandler> Builds transport-specific dispatch handlers from the open-generic consumer/message type pairs collected during registration. A transport implements this to bind a registration to its own delivery closure; MessageExecutionRegistry<THandler> calls it while assembling execution plans. IPublishFilter A filter in the publish/send pipeline. Filters wrap the transport send, allowing cross-cutting concerns (transactional outbox capture, logging, validation, telemetry, …) to be composed without modifying transport or caller code. ITransportPublisher A transport's raw publish terminal: it builds the wire envelope and sends it to the broker. The public IPublisher registered to callers is a filtering facade that runs the publish pipeline and then delegates to this terminal. Transports register their publisher under this interface instead of IPublisher. ITransportSendEndpointProvider A transport's raw send-endpoint provider. The public ISendEndpointProvider registered to callers is a filtering facade that wraps the returned ISendEndpoint with the publish pipeline. Transports register their provider under this interface instead of ISendEndpointProvider. Enums HandlerKind Classifies a dispatch handler by its consumer contract. PublishKind Identifies whether an outgoing message is being broadcast over an exchange (publish) or delivered to a single addressable destination (send). Delegates PublishFilterDelegate Delegate that invokes the next stage in the publish/send pipeline. The terminal stage performs the actual transport publish or send.

api/Vulthil.Messaging.html.title: Namespace Vulthil.Messaging | Vulthil.SharedKernel
api/Vulthil.Messaging.html.summary: Namespace Vulthil.Messaging Classes ConsumeFilterOptions Configures the built-in consume filters that AddMessaging registers by default. Each flag toggles a specific filter on or off; setting a flag to false causes the filter to pass deliveries straight through without performing its work. DependencyInjection Provides extension methods for registering messaging services. MessageConfiguration Configuration used when publishing or binding a message type. Contains exchange declaration settings, the stable wire URN, and optional formatters for routing keys and correlation ids. MessageConfiguration<TMessage> Configuration options for a specific message type, including exchange, routing key and correlation ID selection. PartitionSpec Records how a message type is partitioned for ordered consumption: the Partitioner whose lanes serialize same-key processing, and the typed key selector (held as a Delegate because the message type is only known generically at registration). The transport resolves the concrete selector via the registered message type. Partitioner Distributes work across a fixed number of ordered partitions ("lanes"), selected by a hash of a partition key. Work submitted for the same key runs strictly sequentially and in submission order; work for keys that hash to different lanes runs concurrently. Interfaces IMessageConfigurationProvider Read-only view of the messaging options resolved at runtime by transports, consumers, and filters. Backed by the same instance that IMessagingOptionsConfigurator writes to during composition. IMessagingConfigurator Fluent configurator for registering messaging queues, consumers, and options. IMessagingOptionsConfigurator Mutable view of the messaging options used during composition. Passed to the action of ConfigureMessagingOptions(Action<IMessagingOptionsConfigurator>). ITransport Represents the message transport responsible for starting consumer connections. Enums MessagingExchangeType Specifies values for MessagingExchangeType.

Returns Task<IDbTransaction> A transaction that can be committed or rolled back. ExecuteInTransactionAsync<TResult>(Func<CancellationToken, Task<TResult>>, Func<TResult, bool>, CancellationToken) Runs operation inside a database transaction, committing only when shouldCommit returns true for the produced result and rolling back otherwise (or when operation throws), using the store's execution strategy. Task<TResult> ExecuteInTransactionAsync<TResult>(Func<CancellationToken, Task<TResult>> operation, Func<TResult, bool> shouldCommit, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters operation Func<CancellationToken, Task<TResult>> The work to run inside the transaction. shouldCommit Func<TResult, bool> A predicate that decides, from the produced result, whether to commit the transaction. cancellationToken CancellationToken A token to observe for cancellation. Returns Task<TResult> The result produced by operation. Type Parameters TResult The type produced by operation. Remarks Use this overload to roll back on a returned failure — for example a failed Result from a command handler — rather than only on a thrown exception. The same execution-strategy and ambient-transaction semantics as ExecuteInTransactionAsync<TResult>(Func<CancellationToken, Task<TResult>>, CancellationToken) apply; when a transaction is already active the outer scope owns the commit and shouldCommit is not consulted. ExecuteInTransactionAsync<TResult>(Func<CancellationToken, Task<TResult>>, CancellationToken) Runs operation inside a database transaction, committing on success and rolling back on failure, using the store's execution strategy so it is compatible with a retrying execution strategy. Task<TResult> ExecuteInTransactionAsync<TResult>(Func<CancellationToken, Task<TResult>> operation, CancellationToken cancellationToken) Parameters operation Func<CancellationToken, Task<TResult>> The work to run inside the transaction. cancellationToken CancellationToken A token to observe for cancellation. Returns Task<TResult> The result produced by operation. Type Parameters TResult The type produced by operation. Remarks Prefer this over manually pairing BeginTransactionAsync(CancellationToken) with a commit when a retrying execution strategy may be configured (e.g. the EF Core default): a bare user-initiated transaction is rejected under a retrying strategy, whereas this wraps the whole begin/operation/commit as one retriable unit. A transient-fault retry re-runs operation from a clean change-tracker state, so it must be idempotent. If a transaction is already active, operation simply joins it (the outer scope owns the commit), so this composes with an outer caller that already opened a transaction. SaveChangesAsync(CancellationToken) Persists all pending changes to the underlying store. Task<int> SaveChangesAsync(CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters cancellationToken CancellationToken A token to observe for cancellation. Returns Task<int> The number of state entries written.

api/Vulthil.SharedKernel.Infrastructure..title: Class CosmosOutboxStore<TContext> | Vulthil.SharedKernel
api/Vulthil.SharedKernel.Infrastructure..summary: Class CosmosOutboxStore<TContext> Namespace Vulthil.SharedKernel.Infrastructure.Cosmos.OutboxProcessing Assembly Vulthil.SharedKernel.Infrastructure.Cosmos.dll Cosmos outbox store. Cosmos DB has no cross-partition transaction, so the relay runs without an ambient transaction (best-effort): it uses the base LINQ fetch and materialized update. BaseDbContext(DbContextOptions) Abstract base DbContext that implements IUnitOfWork and ISaveOutboxMessages for outbox integration. protected BaseDbContext(DbContextOptions options) Parameters options DbContextOptions The options for configuring the context. Properties ConfigurationAssembly Gets the assembly containing EF Core entity type configurations to apply during model creation. Return null to skip automatic configuration scanning. protected abstract Assembly? ConfigurationAssembly { get; } Property Value Assembly ConfigurationTypeConstraints Gets an optional type filter predicate applied when scanning for entity type configurations. When null, all configurations in ConfigurationAssembly are applied. protected virtual Func<Type, bool>? ConfigurationTypeConstraints { get; } Property Value Func<Type, bool> IsInTransaction Gets a value indicating whether the persistence context currently has an active database transaction. The transactional outbox uses this to decide whether an outgoing message should be captured into the outbox (atomically with the in-flight business changes) or published directly. public bool IsInTransaction { get; } Property Value bool OutboxMessages Gets the OutboxMessage set tracked by the persistence context. Used by the outbox processor to read, update, and add outbox messages. public DbSet<OutboxMessage> OutboxMessages { get; } Property Value DbSet<OutboxMessage> Methods BeginTransactionAsync(CancellationToken) Begins a new database transaction. public Task<IDbTransaction> BeginTransactionAsync(CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters cancellationToken CancellationToken A token to observe for cancellation. Returns Task<IDbTransaction> A transaction that can be committed or rolled back. ExecuteInTransactionAsync<TResult>(Func<CancellationToken, Task<TResult>>, Func<TResult, bool>, CancellationToken) Runs operation inside a database transaction, committing only when shouldCommit returns true for the produced result and rolling back otherwise (or when operation throws), using the store's execution strategy. public Task<TResult> ExecuteInTransactionAsync<TResult>(Func<CancellationToken, Task<TResult>> operation, Func<TResult, bool> shouldCommit, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters operation Func<CancellationToken, Task<TResult>> The work to run inside the transaction. shouldCommit Func<TResult, bool> A predicate that decides, from the produced result, whether to commit the transaction. cancellationToken CancellationToken A token to observe for cancellation. Returns Task<TResult> The result produced by operation. Type Parameters TResult The type produced by operation. Remarks Use this overload to roll back on a returned failure — for example a failed Result from a command handler — rather than only on a thrown exception. The same execution-strategy and ambient-transaction semantics as ExecuteInTransactionAsync<TResult>(Func<CancellationToken, Task<TResult>>, CancellationToken) apply; when a transaction is already active the outer scope owns the commit and shouldCommit is not consulted. ExecuteInTransactionAsync<TResult>(Func<CancellationToken, Task<TResult>>, CancellationToken) Runs operation inside a database transaction, committing on success and rolling back on failure, using the store's execution strategy so it is compatible with a retrying execution strategy. public Task<TResult> ExecuteInTransactionAsync<TResult>(Func<CancellationToken, Task<TResult>> operation, CancellationToken cancellationToken) Parameters operation Func<CancellationToken, Task<TResult>> The work to run inside the transaction. cancellationToken CancellationToken A token to observe for cancellation. Returns Task<TResult> The result produced by operation. Type Parameters TResult The type produced by operation. Remarks Prefer this over manually pairing BeginTransactionAsync(CancellationToken) with a commit when a retrying execution strategy may be configured (e.g. the EF Core default): a bare user-initiated transaction is rejected under a retrying strategy, whereas this wraps the whole begin/operation/commit as one retriable unit. A transient-fault retry re-runs operation from a clean change-tracker state, so it must be idempotent. If a transaction is already active, operation simply joins it (the outer scope owns the commit), so this composes with an outer caller that already opened a transaction. OnModelCreating(ModelBuilder) Override this method to further configure the model that was discovered by convention from the entity types exposed in DbSet<TEntity> properties on your derived context. The resulting model may be cached and re-used for subsequent instances of your derived context. protected override void OnModelCreating(ModelBuilder modelBuilder) Parameters modelBuilder ModelBuilder The builder being used to construct the model for this context. Databases (and other extensions) typically define extension methods on this object that allow you to configure aspects of the model that are specific to a given database. Remarks If a model is explicitly set on the options for this context (via UseModel(IModel)) then this method will not be run. However, it will still run when creating a compiled model. See Modeling entity types and relationships for more information and examples.
