api/Vulthil.SharedKernel.Infrastructure..summary: Class DatabaseInfrastructureConfigurator<TDbContext> Namespace Vulthil.SharedKernel.Infrastructure Assembly Vulthil.SharedKernel.Infrastructure.dll Fluent configurator for database infrastructure including outbox processing. The DbContext itself is registered by provider-specific extensions (e.g. UseNpgsql) via the OnConfigured(Action<IDatabaseInfrastructureConfigurator<TDbContext>>) hook, not by this class. public sealed class DatabaseInfrastructureConfigurator<TDbContext> : IDatabaseInfrastructureConfigurator<TDbContext> where TDbContext : DbContext Type Parameters TDbContext Inheritance object DatabaseInfrastructureConfigurator<TDbContext> Implements IDatabaseInfrastructureConfigurator<TDbContext> Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Extension Methods ResultExtensions.ToResult<T>(T?, Error) Properties HostApplicationBuilder Provides access to the host application builder for configuring services and settings. public IHostApplicationBuilder HostApplicationBuilder { get; } Property Value IHostApplicationBuilder OutboxOptionsAction Gets the outbox processing options configuration action, or null if not set. public Action<OutboxProcessingOptions>? OutboxOptionsAction { get; } Property Value Action<OutboxProcessingOptions> OutboxProcessingEnabled Gets a value indicating whether outbox processing has been enabled on this configurator. Provider extensions can read this inside an OnConfigured(Action<IDatabaseInfrastructureConfigurator<TDbContext>>) callback to make decisions that depend on the final state of the configuration (for example, enforcing settings that are required for the outbox transaction strategy to work). public bool OutboxProcessingEnabled { get; } Property Value bool OutboxStoreCustomized Gets a value indicating whether an outbox store was explicitly selected via UseOutboxStore<TStore>() while the configurator chain executed. Provider extensions read this inside an OnConfigured(Action<IDatabaseInfrastructureConfigurator<TDbContext>>) callback to apply their store only as a default, so a store the user chose is never overwritten regardless of chaining order. public bool OutboxStoreCustomized { get; } Property Value bool Methods EnableOutboxProcessing(Action<OutboxProcessingOptions>?) Enables outbox processing with optional configuration. public IDatabaseInfrastructureConfigurator<TDbContext> EnableOutboxProcessing(Action<OutboxProcessingOptions>? optionsAction = null) Parameters optionsAction Action<OutboxProcessingOptions> An optional action to configure outbox processing options. Returns IDatabaseInfrastructureConfigurator<TDbContext> The current configurator instance for chaining. OnConfigured(Action<IDatabaseInfrastructureConfigurator<TDbContext>>) Registers a callback that runs once the configurator action has finished executing but before the DbContext is registered with the service collection. Use this hook from provider-specific extension methods (e.g. UseNpgsql) to defer work that depends on the final state of the configurator — most notably whether outbox processing has been enabled — regardless of the order in which the user chained calls. public IDatabaseInfrastructureConfigurator<TDbContext> OnConfigured(Action<IDatabaseInfrastructureConfigurator<TDbContext>> action) Parameters action Action<IDatabaseInfrastructureConfigurator<TDbContext>> The callback to invoke. It receives the configurator in its final state. Returns IDatabaseInfrastructureConfigurator<TDbContext> The current configurator instance for chaining. UseOutboxStore<TStore>() Configures the database infrastructure to use the specified outbox store. public IDatabaseInfrastructureConfigurator<TDbContext> UseOutboxStore<TStore>() where TStore : class, IOutboxStore Returns IDatabaseInfrastructureConfigurator<TDbContext> The database infrastructure configurator instance. Type Parameters TStore The type of outbox store to use.

api/Vulthil.SharedKernel.Infrastructure..title: Class MySqlOutboxStore<TContext> | Vulthil.SharedKernel
api/Vulthil.SharedKernel.Infrastructure..summary: Class MySqlOutboxStore<TContext> Namespace Vulthil.SharedKernel.Infrastructure.MySql.OutboxProcessing Assembly Vulthil.SharedKernel.Infrastructure.MySql.dll MySQL-optimized outbox store that fetches messages with SELECT ... FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED so multiple processors can drain distinct rows concurrently. The fetch SQL is composed from the model's mapped table and column names, so custom identifiers (a naming convention, ToTable, or HasColumnName) are supported, and the composed query carries an explicit outer ORDER BY so dispatch order is deterministic. public class MySqlOutboxStore<TContext> : RelationalOutboxStore<TContext>, IOutboxStore, IOutboxRetentionStore where TContext : DbContext, ISaveOutboxMessages Type Parameters TContext The application's DbContext, which exposes the outbox set. api/Vulthil.SharedKernel.Infrastructure..title: Class DependencyInjectionExtensions | Vulthil.SharedKernel
api/Vulthil.SharedKernel.Infrastructure..summary: Class DependencyInjectionExtensions Namespace Vulthil.SharedKernel.Infrastructure.Npgsql Assembly Vulthil.SharedKernel.Infrastructure.Npgsql.dll Extensions to configure provider-specific behavior for database infrastructure when using PostgreSQL. public static class DependencyInjectionExtensions Inheritance object DependencyInjectionExtensions Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Methods UseNpgsql<TDbContext>(IDatabaseInfrastructureConfigurator<TDbContext>, string, Action<NpgsqlEntityFrameworkCorePostgreSQLSettings>?, Action<DbContextOptionsBuilder>?) Configures the database infrastructure to use the PostgreSQL-optimized outbox strategy and registers an Aspire-wired DbContext for the connection string key. public static IDatabaseInfrastructureConfigurator<TDbContext> UseNpgsql<TDbContext>(this IDatabaseInfrastructureConfigurator<TDbContext> configurator, string connectionStringKey, Action<NpgsqlEntityFrameworkCorePostgreSQLSettings>? configureSettings = null, Action<DbContextOptionsBuilder>? configureDbContextOptions = null) where TDbContext : DbContext, ISaveOutboxMessages Parameters configurator IDatabaseInfrastructureConfigurator<TDbContext> The database infrastructure configurator. connectionStringKey string The key for the connection string. configureSettings Action<NpgsqlEntityFrameworkCorePostgreSQLSettings> Optional action to configure PostgreSQL-specific settings. configureDbContextOptions Action<DbContextOptionsBuilder> Optional action to configure the DbContextOptionsBuilder. Returns IDatabaseInfrastructureConfigurator<TDbContext> The configurator for chaining. Type Parameters TDbContext Remarks The actual call to AddNpgsqlDbContext is deferred until the full configurator chain has executed, so the order of UseNpgsql<TDbContext>(IDatabaseInfrastructureConfigurator<TDbContext>, string, Action<NpgsqlEntityFrameworkCorePostgreSQLSettings>?, Action<DbContextOptionsBuilder>?), EnableOutboxProcessing(Action<OutboxProcessingOptions>?), and UseOutboxStore<TStore>() is irrelevant: the PostgreSQL-optimized outbox store is applied as a default, so a custom store selected via UseOutboxStore is preserved no matter where in the chain this method is called. The outbox processor runs its transactional unit inside the context's execution strategy (Database.CreateExecutionStrategy().ExecuteAsync), so a retrying execution strategy is fully supported and there is no need to force DisableRetry. All settings — including CommandTimeout — are left to the caller.

api/Vulthil.SharedKernel.Infrastructure..summary: Class RelationalOutboxStore<TContext> Namespace Vulthil.SharedKernel.Infrastructure.Relational.OutboxProcessing Assembly Vulthil.SharedKernel.Infrastructure.Relational.dll Outbox store for relational database providers. Records processed and failed messages with set-based ExecuteUpdate calls instead of materializing rows. Provider packages (e.g. Npgsql, MySQL) inherit from this class and override FetchMessagesAsync(int, int, CancellationToken) to add row-level locking. public class RelationalOutboxStore<TContext> : EntityFrameworkOutboxStore<TContext>, IOutboxStore, IOutboxRetentionStore where TContext : DbContext, ISaveOutboxMessages Type Parameters TContext The application's DbContext, which exposes the outbox set. Inheritance object EntityFrameworkOutboxStore<TContext> RelationalOutboxStore<TContext> Implements IOutboxStore IOutboxRetentionStore Derived MySqlOutboxStore<TContext> NpgsqlOutboxStore<TContext> Inherited Members EntityFrameworkOutboxStore<TContext>.DbContext EntityFrameworkOutboxStore<TContext>.Logger EntityFrameworkOutboxStore<TContext>.OutboxMessages EntityFrameworkOutboxStore<TContext>.IsInTransaction EntityFrameworkOutboxStore<TContext>.AddOutboxMessage(OutboxMessage) EntityFrameworkOutboxStore<TContext>.SaveChangesAsync(CancellationToken) EntityFrameworkOutboxStore<TContext>.ProcessBatchAsync(Func<OutboxMessageData, CancellationToken, Task<string>>, CancellationToken) EntityFrameworkOutboxStore<TContext>.FetchMessagesAsync(int, int, CancellationToken) object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Extension Methods ResultExtensions.ToResult<T>(T?, Error) Constructors RelationalOutboxStore(TContext, TimeProvider, IOptions<OutboxProcessingOptions>) Outbox store for relational database providers. Records processed and failed messages with set-based ExecuteUpdate calls instead of materializing rows. Provider packages (e.g. Npgsql, MySQL) inherit from this class and override FetchMessagesAsync(int, int, CancellationToken) to add row-level locking. public RelationalOutboxStore(TContext dbContext, TimeProvider timeProvider, IOptions<OutboxProcessingOptions> options) Parameters dbContext TContext timeProvider TimeProvider options IOptions<OutboxProcessingOptions> Methods BeginTransactionAsync(CancellationToken) Opens the transaction for the relay batch, requiring TContext to support one. protected override Task<IDbTransaction?> BeginTransactionAsync(CancellationToken cancellationToken) Parameters cancellationToken CancellationToken A token to observe for cancellation. Returns Task<IDbTransaction> The transaction to commit on success. Exceptions InvalidOperationException TContext does not implement IUnitOfWork, so no transaction could be opened. DeleteProcessedAsync(DateTimeOffset, int, CancellationToken) Deletes up to batchSize outbox rows that were processed or dead-lettered before olderThanUtc. Pending rows are never deleted. public override Task<int> DeleteProcessedAsync(DateTimeOffset olderThanUtc, int batchSize, CancellationToken cancellationToken) Parameters olderThanUtc DateTimeOffset The cutoff; only terminal rows whose ProcessedOnUtc or FailedOnUtc is older than this are deleted. batchSize int The maximum number of rows to delete in this call. cancellationToken CancellationToken A token to observe for cancellation. Returns Task<int> The number of rows deleted. Remarks Selects the keys of the oldest eligible rows first and deletes by key, so a single call never removes more than batchSize rows — a large backlog is drained in bounded, short-lived deletes instead of one long-locking statement. UpdateMessagesAsync(IReadOnlyList<Guid>, IReadOnlyList<OutboxMessageFailure>, int, DateTimeOffset, CancellationToken) Records processed and failed messages, dead-lettering any that reach maxRetries. The default fetches every affected row in a single query, applies the outcome in memory, and saves once; relational providers override this with set-based ExecuteUpdate calls. protected override Task UpdateMessagesAsync(IReadOnlyList<Guid> successIds, IReadOnlyList<OutboxMessageFailure> failures, int maxRetries, DateTimeOffset processedOnUtc, CancellationToken cancellationToken) Parameters successIds IReadOnlyList<Guid> Identifiers of successfully delivered messages. failures IReadOnlyList<OutboxMessageFailure> Failures to record with a retry increment. maxRetries int Failed messages reaching this retry count are dead-lettered (their FailedOnUtc is set). processedOnUtc DateTimeOffset The timestamp to record for processed or dead-lettered messages. cancellationToken CancellationToken A token to observe for cancellation. Returns Task

api/Vulthil.SharedKernel.Outbox.EntityFr.summary: Class EntityFrameworkOutboxStore<TContext> Namespace Vulthil.SharedKernel.Outbox.EntityFrameworkCore Assembly Vulthil.SharedKernel.Outbox.EntityFrameworkCore.dll Entity Framework Core implementation of IOutboxStore. It owns the relay batch unit — runs inside the context's execution strategy, opens the transaction, fetches a batch, dispatches each message, records the outcome, and commits — and exposes the capture surface used by bus-publish filters. Provider packages derive from this type to add row-level locking (FetchMessagesAsync(int, int, CancellationToken)), set-based updates (UpdateMessagesAsync(IReadOnlyList<Guid>, IReadOnlyList<OutboxMessageFailure>, int, DateTimeOffset, CancellationToken)), or a no-op transaction (BeginTransactionAsync(CancellationToken)). public class EntityFrameworkOutboxStore<TContext> : IOutboxStore, IOutboxRetentionStore where TContext : DbContext, ISaveOutboxMessages Type Parameters TContext The application's DbContext, which exposes the outbox set. Inheritance object EntityFrameworkOutboxStore<TContext> Implements IOutboxStore IOutboxRetentionStore Derived CosmosOutboxStore<TContext> RelationalOutboxStore<TContext> Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Extension Methods ResultExtensions.ToResult<T>(T?, Error) Constructors EntityFrameworkOutboxStore(TContext, TimeProvider, IOptions<OutboxProcessingOptions>) Initializes a new instance of the EntityFrameworkOutboxStore<TContext> class. public EntityFrameworkOutboxStore(TContext dbContext, TimeProvider timeProvider, IOptions<OutboxProcessingOptions> options) Parameters dbContext TContext The application's persistence context. timeProvider TimeProvider The time provider used to stamp processed messages. options IOptions<OutboxProcessingOptions> The outbox processing options (batch size, retry limit, parallelism). Properties DbContext Gets the application's persistence context. protected TContext DbContext { get; } Property Value TContext IsInTransaction Gets a value indicating whether a database transaction is currently active. Capture uses this to decide whether an outgoing message is enlisted in the ambient transaction or published directly. public bool IsInTransaction { get; } Property Value bool Logger Gets a logger resolved from the persistence context's logging integration. protected ILogger Logger { get; } Property Value ILogger OutboxMessages Gets the tracked outbox message set. protected DbSet<OutboxMessage> OutboxMessages { get; } Property Value DbSet<OutboxMessage> Methods AddOutboxMessage(OutboxMessage) Stages an outbox message for persistence. Used by capture (e.g. a transactional bus-publish filter) to enlist the message in the ambient business transaction; call SaveChangesAsync(CancellationToken) to flush it. public void AddOutboxMessage(OutboxMessage message) Parameters message OutboxMessage The message to stage. BeginTransactionAsync(CancellationToken) Begins the transaction for the relay batch. The default enlists the context's IUnitOfWork; providers without ambient transactions (e.g. Cosmos) override this. protected virtual Task<IDbTransaction?> BeginTransactionAsync(CancellationToken cancellationToken) Parameters cancellationToken CancellationToken A token to observe for cancellation. Returns Task<IDbTransaction> The transaction to commit on success, or null when none is used. DeleteProcessedAsync(DateTimeOffset, int, CancellationToken) Deletes up to batchSize outbox rows that were processed or dead-lettered before olderThanUtc. Pending rows are never deleted. public virtual Task<int> DeleteProcessedAsync(DateTimeOffset olderThanUtc, int batchSize, CancellationToken cancellationToken) Parameters olderThanUtc DateTimeOffset The cutoff; only terminal rows whose ProcessedOnUtc or FailedOnUtc is older than this are deleted. batchSize int The maximum number of rows to delete in this call. cancellationToken CancellationToken A token to observe for cancellation. Returns Task<int> The number of rows deleted. FetchMessagesAsync(int, int, CancellationToken) Fetches a batch of unprocessed messages. Providers override this to add row-level locking (e.g. FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED). protected virtual Task<List<OutboxMessageData>> FetchMessagesAsync(int batchSize, int maxRetries, CancellationToken cancellationToken) Parameters batchSize int The maximum number of messages to fetch. maxRetries int Messages at or above this retry count are excluded. cancellationToken CancellationToken A token to observe for cancellation. Returns Task<List<OutboxMessageData>> The fetched message data. ProcessBatchAsync(Func<OutboxMessageData, CancellationToken, Task<string?>>, CancellationToken) Processes one batch of pending outbox messages: fetches up to the configured batch size, invokes dispatch for each, records the outcome (success, or the returned error with a retry increment), and commits — all within the store's transactional boundary. public Task<int> ProcessBatchAsync(Func<OutboxMessageData, CancellationToken, Task<string?>> dispatch, CancellationToken cancellationToken) Parameters dispatch Func<OutboxMessageData, CancellationToken, Task<string>> Delivers a single message; returns null on success, or an error description to record against the row as a failed attempt. cancellationToken CancellationToken A token to observe for cancellation. Returns Task<int> The number of messages dispatch reported as successful. A caller uses this to decide whether more work is likely waiting (the count reaches the configured batch size) — a batch that included failures must not be mistaken for one that is exhausted. SaveChangesAsync(CancellationToken) Persists pending staged outbox messages. public Task<int> SaveChangesAsync(CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters cancellationToken CancellationToken A token to observe for cancellation. Returns Task<int> The number of state entries written. UpdateMessagesAsync(IReadOnlyList<Guid>, IReadOnlyList<OutboxMessageFailure>, int, DateTimeOffset, CancellationToken) Records processed and failed messages, dead-lettering any that reach maxRetries. The default fetches every affected row in a single query, applies the outcome in memory, and saves once; relational providers override this with set-based ExecuteUpdate calls. protected virtual Task UpdateMessagesAsync(IReadOnlyList<Guid> successIds, IReadOnlyList<OutboxMessageFailure> failures, int maxRetries, DateTimeOffset processedOnUtc, CancellationToken cancellationToken) Parameters successIds IReadOnlyList<Guid> Identifiers of successfully delivered messages. failures IReadOnlyList<OutboxMessageFailure> Failures to record with a retry increment. maxRetries int Failed messages reaching this retry count are dead-lettered (their FailedOnUtc is set). processedOnUtc DateTimeOffset The timestamp to record for processed or dead-lettered messages. cancellationToken CancellationToken A token to observe for cancellation. Returns Task

Implementations are resolved from DI and added to the context options when outbox processing is enabled, so a provider package (e.g. relational) can contribute a transaction-commit interceptor without the provider-agnostic base needing to reference relational-only interceptor types. api/Vulthil.SharedKernel.Outbox.IOutboxD.title: Interface IOutboxDispatcher | Vulthil.SharedKernel
api/Vulthil.SharedKernel.Outbox.IOutboxD.summary: Interface IOutboxDispatcher Namespace Vulthil.SharedKernel.Outbox Assembly Vulthil.SharedKernel.Outbox.dll Relays a fetched outbox message to its sink. The Vulthil.SharedKernel.Outbox.OutboxProcessor routes each row to the registered dispatcher whose Handles(OutboxDestination) returns true for the row's OutboxDestination, so a single outbox table can feed several sinks (in-process domain events, broker publishes/sends) that coexist in one application. public interface IOutboxDispatcher Extension Methods ResultExtensions.ToResult<T>(T?, Error) Methods DispatchAsync(OutboxMessageData, CancellationToken) Relays message to its sink. Task DispatchAsync(OutboxMessageData message, CancellationToken cancellationToken) Parameters message OutboxMessageData The fetched outbox message data. cancellationToken CancellationToken A token to observe for cancellation. Returns Task Handles(OutboxDestination) Whether this dispatcher relays messages for the given destination. bool Handles(OutboxDestination destination) Parameters destination OutboxDestination The message's destination. Returns bool

api/Vulthil.SharedKernel.Outbox.IOutboxR.title: Interface IOutboxRelayGate | Vulthil.SharedKernel
api/Vulthil.SharedKernel.Outbox.IOutboxR.summary: Interface IOutboxRelayGate Namespace Vulthil.SharedKernel.Outbox Assembly Vulthil.SharedKernel.Outbox.dll A readiness gate the outbox relay awaits once before its first processing pass. It lets a downstream sink defer the relay until its delivery channel is ready — for example, the message-broker sink waits for the transport to declare its subscriber topology, so a relayed publish is not lost by being sent before the subscriber queues are bound. When no gate is registered the relay starts immediately. public interface IOutboxRelayGate Extension Methods ResultExtensions.ToResult<T>(T?, Error) Methods WaitUntilReadyAsync(CancellationToken) Completes when the gate's downstream channel is ready to accept relayed messages. Task WaitUntilReadyAsync(CancellationToken cancellationToken) Parameters cancellationToken CancellationToken A token to observe for cancellation. Returns Task A task that completes when the relay may begin processing.

api/Vulthil.SharedKernel.Outbox.IOutboxR.title: Interface IOutboxRetentionStore | Vulthil.SharedKernel
api/Vulthil.SharedKernel.Outbox.IOutboxR.summary: Interface IOutboxRetentionStore Namespace Vulthil.SharedKernel.Outbox Assembly Vulthil.SharedKernel.Outbox.dll An IOutboxStore that can purge old terminal (processed or dead-lettered) rows for the retention sweep. The EF Core outbox store implements this; the retention background service skips the sweep when the registered store does not. public interface IOutboxRetentionStore Extension Methods ResultExtensions.ToResult<T>(T?, Error) Methods DeleteProcessedAsync(DateTimeOffset, int, CancellationToken) Deletes up to batchSize outbox rows that were processed or dead-lettered before olderThanUtc. Pending rows are never deleted. Task<int> DeleteProcessedAsync(DateTimeOffset olderThanUtc, int batchSize, CancellationToken cancellationToken) Parameters olderThanUtc DateTimeOffset The cutoff; only terminal rows whose ProcessedOnUtc or FailedOnUtc is older than this are deleted. batchSize int The maximum number of rows to delete in this call. cancellationToken CancellationToken A token to observe for cancellation. Returns Task<int> The number of rows deleted.

api/Vulthil.SharedKernel.Outbox.IOutboxS.title: Interface IOutboxSignal | Vulthil.SharedKernel
api/Vulthil.SharedKernel.Outbox.IOutboxS.summary: Interface IOutboxSignal Namespace Vulthil.SharedKernel.Outbox Assembly Vulthil.SharedKernel.Outbox.dll Wakes the outbox background service when a transaction commits, so freshly-written outbox messages are relayed promptly instead of waiting for the next poll. Signals coalesce (at most one pending wake), and the periodic poll remains the correctness backstop for retries, other instances, and missed signals. public interface IOutboxSignal Extension Methods ResultExtensions.ToResult<T>(T?, Error) Methods Notify() Signals that outbox work may be available, releasing a waiter if one is pending. void Notify() WaitAsync(TimeSpan, CancellationToken) Waits until Notify() is called or timeout elapses. Task WaitAsync(TimeSpan timeout, CancellationToken cancellationToken) Parameters timeout TimeSpan The maximum time to wait before returning regardless of a signal. cancellationToken CancellationToken A token to observe for cancellation. Returns Task

api/Vulthil.SharedKernel.Outbox.IOutboxS.title: Interface IOutboxStore | Vulthil.SharedKernel
api/Vulthil.SharedKernel.Outbox.IOutboxS.summary: Interface IOutboxStore Namespace Vulthil.SharedKernel.Outbox Assembly Vulthil.SharedKernel.Outbox.dll Persistence-agnostic store the outbox engine relies on for both capture and relay. A relational or other EF Core implementation lives in Vulthil.SharedKernel.Outbox.EntityFrameworkCore and its provider packages; the engine itself takes no dependency on EF Core. public interface IOutboxStore Extension Methods ResultExtensions.ToResult<T>(T?, Error) Remarks The store owns the whole relay batch unit — provider locking, the transactional boundary, fetching the pending rows, dispatching each, recording success/failure, and committing — so a relational implementation can run it inside an EF Core execution strategy. The capture members are used by message-bridge publish filters to enlist an outgoing message in the ambient business transaction. Properties IsInTransaction Gets a value indicating whether a database transaction is currently active. Capture uses this to decide whether an outgoing message is enlisted in the ambient transaction or published directly. bool IsInTransaction { get; } Property Value bool Methods AddOutboxMessage(OutboxMessage) Stages an outbox message for persistence. Used by capture (e.g. a transactional bus-publish filter) to enlist the message in the ambient business transaction; call SaveChangesAsync(CancellationToken) to flush it. void AddOutboxMessage(OutboxMessage message) Parameters message OutboxMessage The message to stage. ProcessBatchAsync(Func<OutboxMessageData, CancellationToken, Task<string?>>, CancellationToken) Processes one batch of pending outbox messages: fetches up to the configured batch size, invokes dispatch for each, records the outcome (success, or the returned error with a retry increment), and commits — all within the store's transactional boundary. Task<int> ProcessBatchAsync(Func<OutboxMessageData, CancellationToken, Task<string?>> dispatch, CancellationToken cancellationToken) Parameters dispatch Func<OutboxMessageData, CancellationToken, Task<string>> Delivers a single message; returns null on success, or an error description to record against the row as a failed attempt. cancellationToken CancellationToken A token to observe for cancellation. Returns Task<int> The number of messages dispatch reported as successful. A caller uses this to decide whether more work is likely waiting (the count reaches the configured batch size) — a batch that included failures must not be mistaken for one that is exhausted. SaveChangesAsync(CancellationToken) Persists pending staged outbox messages. Task<int> SaveChangesAsync(CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters cancellationToken CancellationToken A token to observe for cancellation. Returns Task<int> The number of state entries written.

api/Vulthil.SharedKernel.Outbox.MeterPro.summary: Class MeterProviderBuilderExtensions Namespace Vulthil.SharedKernel.Outbox Assembly Vulthil.SharedKernel.Outbox.dll Extension methods for subscribing a OpenTelemetry.Metrics.MeterProviderBuilder to the outbox relay metrics. public static class MeterProviderBuilderExtensions Inheritance object MeterProviderBuilderExtensions Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Methods AddVulthilOutboxInstrumentation(MeterProviderBuilder) Subscribes the meter provider to the Meter emitted by the outbox relay (the vulthil.outbox.* counters), so outbox metrics are collected without hard-coding the meter name. public static MeterProviderBuilder AddVulthilOutboxInstrumentation(this MeterProviderBuilder builder) Parameters builder MeterProviderBuilder The meter provider builder. Returns MeterProviderBuilder The same builder, for chaining.

api/Vulthil.SharedKernel.Outbox.OutboxDe.title: Enum OutboxDestination | Vulthil.SharedKernel
api/Vulthil.SharedKernel.Outbox.OutboxDe.summary: Enum OutboxDestination Namespace Vulthil.SharedKernel.Outbox Assembly Vulthil.SharedKernel.Outbox.dll Identifies the sink an outbox message is relayed to, so the Vulthil.SharedKernel.Outbox.OutboxProcessor can route each row to the matching IOutboxDispatcher. One outbox table can carry messages bound for different sinks. public enum OutboxDestination Fields DomainEvent = 0 An in-process domain event, dispatched to IDomainEventHandler implementations. Publish = 1 A message published to the broker (pub/sub). Send = 2 A message sent point-to-point to a broker endpoint.

api/Vulthil.SharedKernel.Outbox.OutboxEn.title: Class OutboxEngineServiceCollectionExtensions | Vulthil.SharedKernel
api/Vulthil.SharedKernel.Outbox.OutboxEn.summary: Class OutboxEngineServiceCollectionExtensions Namespace Vulthil.SharedKernel.Outbox Assembly Vulthil.SharedKernel.Outbox.dll Service-collection extensions that register the provider-agnostic outbox engine — the relay processor, the background service, the commit-time signal, and the in-process domain-event sink. A persistence host (e.g. Vulthil.SharedKernel.Infrastructure) calls this and then registers an IOutboxStore implementation for its provider. public static class OutboxEngineServiceCollectionExtensions Inheritance object OutboxEngineServiceCollectionExtensions Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Methods AddOutboxEngine(IServiceCollection, Action<OutboxProcessingOptions>?, ServiceLifetime) Registers the outbox engine: OutboxProcessingOptions (validated on start), the commit-time IOutboxSignal, the relay Vulthil.SharedKernel.Outbox.OutboxProcessor and its background service, and the default in-process IOutboxDispatcher for domain events. An IOutboxStore must be registered separately by the persistence provider. The engine's own services are registered idempotently, so calling this repeatedly (e.g. once per EnableOutboxProcessing call) never duplicates them; only one IOutboxStore is supported per host, because the relay and retention services resolve a single instance, so a second one being already registered fails fast instead of orphaning the first context's outbox. public static IServiceCollection AddOutboxEngine(this IServiceCollection services, Action<OutboxProcessingOptions>? configureOptions = null, ServiceLifetime processorLifetime = ServiceLifetime.Scoped) Parameters services IServiceCollection The service collection. configureOptions Action<OutboxProcessingOptions> An optional action to configure OutboxProcessingOptions. processorLifetime ServiceLifetime The lifetime for the relay processor and the in-process dispatcher; match the application's DbContext lifetime so they resolve the store in the same scope. Returns IServiceCollection The same service collection, for chaining. Exceptions InvalidOperationException An IOutboxStore is already registered for a different context.

api/Vulthil.SharedKernel.Outbox.OutboxMe.summary: Class OutboxMessage Namespace Vulthil.SharedKernel.Outbox Assembly Vulthil.SharedKernel.Outbox.dll Represents a serialized domain event stored for reliable outbox-based delivery. public sealed class OutboxMessage Inheritance object OutboxMessage Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Extension Methods ResultExtensions.ToResult<T>(T?, Error) Properties Content Gets the JSON-serialized domain event content. public required string Content { get; init; } Property Value string Destination Gets the sink this message is relayed to (in-process domain event, broker publish, or broker send). Error { get; set; } Property Value string FailedOnUtc Gets or sets the UTC timestamp when the message was dead-lettered after exhausting its retries, or null if it has not permanently failed. TraceParent { get; set; } Property Value string TraceState Activity TraceState, for distributed tracing correlation. public string? api/Vulthil.SharedKernel.Outbox.OutboxPr.summary: Class OutboxProcessingOptions Namespace Vulthil.SharedKernel.Outbox Assembly Vulthil.SharedKernel.Outbox.dll Configuration options for the outbox background processing service. public sealed class OutboxProcessingOptions Inheritance object OutboxProcessingOptions Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Extension Methods ResultExtensions.ToResult<T>(T?, Error) Properties BatchSize Gets the maximum number of outbox messages to process per cycle. Default is 10. [Range(1, 2147483647)] public int BatchSize { get; set; } Property Value int EnableMetrics Gets or sets a value indicating whether outbox relay metrics (the vulthil.outbox.* counters on the MeterName meter) are auto-registered with OpenTelemetry. Default: true. public bool EnableMetrics { get; set; } Property Value bool EnableParallelPublishing Gets a value indicating whether messages within a batch should be published in parallel. When enabled, each message is dispatched in its own dependency-injection scope so handlers do not share the relay's DbContext, and concurrency is bounded by MaxDegreeOfParallelism. public bool EnableParallelPublishing { get; set; } Property Value bool EnableTracing Gets or sets a value indicating whether Trace Identifiers should be included when publishing outbox messages. This allows the originating action that persisted the outbox message to act as an owner for the resulting scope, even after the outbox delay Default: true public bool EnableTracing { get; set; } Property Value bool MaxDegreeOfParallelism Gets the maximum number of messages dispatched concurrently when EnableParallelPublishing is enabled. Default is 4. Ignored when parallel publishing is disabled. [Range(1, 100)] public int MaxDegreeOfParallelism { get; set; } Property Value int MaxDelaySeconds Gets the maximum back-off delay in seconds when no messages are found. Default is 60. [Range(1, 300)] public int MaxDelaySeconds { get; set; } Property Value int MaxRetries Gets the maximum number of delivery attempts before a message is marked as processed. Default is 3. [Range(1, 2147483647)] public int MaxRetries { get; set; } Property Value int OutboxProcessingDelaySeconds Gets the base polling delay in seconds between outbox processing cycles. Default is 2. [Range(1, 100)] public int OutboxProcessingDelaySeconds { get; set; } Property Value int Retention Gets the retention sweep configuration. Set Enabled to periodically delete processed and dead-lettered rows older than RetentionPeriod. public OutboxRetentionOptions Retention { get; } Property Value OutboxRetentionOptions

api/Vulthil.xUnit.BaseWebApplicationFact.title: Class BaseWebApplicationFactory<TEntryPoint> | Vulthil.SharedKernel
api/Vulthil.xUnit.BaseWebApplicationFact.summary: Class BaseWebApplicationFactory<TEntryPoint> Namespace Vulthil.xUnit Assembly Vulthil.xUnit.dll Abstract WebApplicationFactory<TEntryPoint> that manages a set of test containers, injects their connection strings into the test host, and ensures EF Core migrations are applied during host startup. public abstract class BaseWebApplicationFactory<TEntryPoint> : WebApplicationFactory<TEntryPoint>, IDisposable, IAsyncLifetime, IAsyncDisposable where TEntryPoint : class Type Parameters TEntryPoint Inheritance object WebApplicationFactory<TEntryPoint> BaseWebApplicationFactory<TEntryPoint> Implements IDisposable IAsyncLifetime IAsyncDisposable Inherited Members WebApplicationFactory<TEntryPoint>.WithWebHostBuilder(Action<IWebHostBuilder>) WebApplicationFactory<TEntryPoint>.GetTestAssemblies() WebApplicationFactory<TEntryPoint>.CreateHostBuilder() WebApplicationFactory<TEntryPoint>.CreateWebHostBuilder() WebApplicationFactory<TEntryPoint>.CreateServer(IWebHostBuilder) WebApplicationFactory<TEntryPoint>.CreateHost(IHostBuilder) WebApplicationFactory<TEntryPoint>.CreateClient() WebApplicationFactory<TEntryPoint>.CreateClient(WebApplicationFactoryClientOptions) WebApplicationFactory<TEntryPoint>.CreateDefaultClient(params DelegatingHandler[]) WebApplicationFactory<TEntryPoint>.ConfigureClient(HttpClient) WebApplicationFactory<TEntryPoint>.CreateDefaultClient(Uri, params DelegatingHandler[]) WebApplicationFactory<TEntryPoint>.Dispose() WebApplicationFactory<TEntryPoint>.Dispose(bool) WebApplicationFactory<TEntryPoint>.Server WebApplicationFactory<TEntryPoint>.Services WebApplicationFactory<TEntryPoint>.Factories WebApplicationFactory<TEntryPoint>.ClientOptions object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Extension Methods ResultExtensions.ToResult<T>(T?, Error) Remarks Migrations run from an IHostedService registered at the front of the host's hosted-service list, so the schema exists before the application's own background services start. The migration step only applies migrations that are still pending and tolerates a concurrent migrator, so an application that migrates itself on startup keeps ownership and the factory never interferes with the application's own migration logic. Use a derived factory as an Xunit.IClassFixture<TFixture> (or collection fixture) so its containers are started once and shared across the tests in that scope, while BaseIntegrationTestCase<TFactory, TEntryPoint> resets database state between tests. To share containers across many parallel test classes, register them on a ContainerHost assembly fixture instead and pass it to the BaseWebApplicationFactory(ContainerHost) constructor; the factory then consumes every host container through a per-factory scope view (an isolated database, virtual host, ...) so test classes run in parallel against shared containers without interfering. Constructors BaseWebApplicationFactory() Initializes a factory that owns its containers exclusively; register them with AddContainer(ITestContainer) in the constructor or in ConfigureContainers(). protected BaseWebApplicationFactory() BaseWebApplicationFactory(ContainerHost) Initializes a factory that consumes the shared containers of containerHost. Every container registered on the host is consumed through a per-factory scope view (filter with ShouldUseContainer(ITestContainer)), so no per-factory container registration is needed and parallel test classes share the running containers without sharing state. protected BaseWebApplicationFactory(ContainerHost containerHost) Parameters containerHost ContainerHost The assembly-level host whose containers this factory consumes. Properties ContainersWithConnectionStrings Gets the registered containers that expose a connection string, used for injecting settings into the test host. public IEnumerable<ITestContainerWithConnectionString> ContainersWithConnectionStrings { get; } Property Value IEnumerable<ITestContainerWithConnectionString> DatabaseContainers Gets the registered database containers that support migrations and data resets. public IEnumerable<ITestDatabaseContainer> DatabaseContainers { get; } Property Value IEnumerable<ITestDatabaseContainer> Methods AddContainer(ITestContainer) Registers a test container to be managed by this factory. protected void AddContainer(ITestContainer container) Parameters container ITestContainer The container to register. AddHttpMock(string) Registers an in-process HTTP mock for the named HttpClient registered with AddHttpClient("name"), and routes that client's outbound calls through it by replacing its primary message handler. Configure responses per test via GetHttpMock(string) (or HttpMock("name") on the test case); the mock is reset between tests. protected IHttpMock AddHttpMock(string name) Parameters name string The logical name of the HTTP client to mock. Returns IHttpMock The registered mock, so responses can optionally be configured up front. AddHttpMock<TClient>() Registers an in-process HTTP mock for the typed client TClient (as registered with AddHttpClient<TClient, ...>()), keyed by its logical client name. The implementation type does not need to be accessible. See AddHttpMock(string). protected IHttpMock AddHttpMock<TClient>() where TClient : class Returns IHttpMock The registered mock, so responses can optionally be configured up front. Type Parameters TClient The typed client service type registered with AddHttpClient. ConfigureContainers() Override to register test containers by calling AddContainer(ITestContainer). protected virtual Task ConfigureContainers() Returns Task A task representing the asynchronous registration work. ConfigureCustomWebHost(IWebHostBuilder) Override to apply additional IWebHostBuilder configuration for tests. protected virtual void ConfigureCustomWebHost(IWebHostBuilder builder) Parameters builder IWebHostBuilder The web host builder to configure. ConfigureWebHost(IWebHostBuilder) Gives a fixture an opportunity to configure the application before it gets built. protected override sealed void ConfigureWebHost(IWebHostBuilder builder) Parameters builder IWebHostBuilder The IWebHostBuilder for the application. CreateScopeId() Creates the identifier under which this factory's scopes are isolated inside shared containers (database names, virtual hosts, ...). The default combines the factory type name with a random suffix, yielding a new unique scope per factory instance — that is, per test class when the factory is used as a class fixture. protected virtual string CreateScopeId() Returns string A short, unique, lowercase identifier that is safe to embed in names. DisposeAsync() Performs application-defined tasks associated with freeing, releasing, or resetting unmanaged resources asynchronously. public override ValueTask DisposeAsync() Returns ValueTask A task that represents the asynchronous dispose operation. GetHttpMock(string) Gets the HTTP mock registered for the named HTTP client name. public IHttpMock GetHttpMock(string name) Parameters name string The logical name of the HTTP client. Returns IHttpMock The registered mock. Exceptions InvalidOperationException No mock was registered for name. GetHttpMock<TClient>() Gets the HTTP mock registered for the typed client TClient. public IHttpMock GetHttpMock<TClient>() where TClient : class Returns IHttpMock The registered mock. Type Parameters TClient The typed client service type. Exceptions InvalidOperationException No mock was registered for TClient. InitializeAsync() Registers and initializes every consumed test container in parallel. Invoked once by xUnit before the tests in scope run. Factory-owned containers are started here; containers consumed from a ContainerHost are started on the host (once per test run) and only their per-factory scopes are provisioned here. public ValueTask InitializeAsync() Returns ValueTask A task representing the asynchronous startup work. ShouldUseContainer(ITestContainer) Decides whether a container registered on the ContainerHost is consumed by this factory. All host containers are consumed by default; override to exclude containers a factory's scenario does not use (excluded containers are not started by this factory either, thanks to lazy host startup). protected virtual bool ShouldUseContainer(ITestContainer container) Parameters container ITestContainer A container registered on the host. Returns bool true to consume the container; otherwise false.

api/Vulthil.xUnit.Cosmos.CosmosTestConta.title: Class CosmosTestContainerFixture<TDbContext> | Vulthil.SharedKernel
api/Vulthil.xUnit.Cosmos.CosmosTestConta.summary: Class CosmosTestContainerFixture<TDbContext> Namespace Vulthil.xUnit.Cosmos Assembly Vulthil.xUnit.Cosmos.dll Fixture that starts the Azure Cosmos DB Linux emulator in a container and registers TDbContext in the test host pointed at it. Connectivity goes through the container's own Testcontainers.CosmosDb.CosmosDbContainer.HttpClient, whose handler rewrites every request to the mapped emulator endpoint, so no certificate or endpoint plumbing is needed. When owned by a ContainerHost, every consuming factory works in its own emulator database, so parallel test classes never share data; data is reset between tests by recreating that database. Derived classes only supply the configuration key (and optionally pin the emulator image via Configure). public abstract class CosmosTestContainerFixture<TDbContext> : TestContainerFixtureWithConnectionString<CosmosDbBuilder, CosmosDbContainer>, ITestContainerScopeProvider, ITestContainerWithConnectionString, ITestContainer, IAsyncLifetime, IAsyncDisposable, IStartupResource, IResettableResource where TDbContext : DbContext Type Parameters TDbContext The Cosmos-mapped DbContext to register against the emulator. Inheritance object ContainerLifetime<CosmosDbBuilder, CosmosDbContainer> ContainerFixture<CosmosDbBuilder, CosmosDbContainer> TestContainerFixture<CosmosDbBuilder, CosmosDbContainer> TestContainerFixtureWithConnectionString<CosmosDbBuilder, CosmosDbContainer> CosmosTestContainerFixture<TDbContext> Implements ITestContainerScopeProvider ITestContainerWithConnectionString ITestContainer IAsyncLifetime IAsyncDisposable IStartupResource IResettableResource Inherited Members TestContainerFixtureWithConnectionString<CosmosDbBuilder, CosmosDbContainer>.ConnectionString TestContainerFixtureWithConnectionString<CosmosDbBuilder, CosmosDbContainer>.ConnectionStringKey TestContainerFixtureWithConnectionString<CosmosDbBuilder, CosmosDbContainer>.CreateScope(string) TestContainerFixture<CosmosDbBuilder, CosmosDbContainer>.ConfigureWebHost(IWebHostBuilder) TestContainerFixture<CosmosDbBuilder, CosmosDbContainer>.ConfigureServices(IServiceCollection) ContainerLifetime<CosmosDbBuilder, CosmosDbContainer>.Configure() ContainerLifetime<CosmosDbBuilder, CosmosDbContainer>.Configure(CosmosDbBuilder) ContainerLifetime<CosmosDbBuilder, CosmosDbContainer>.InitializeAsync() ContainerLifetime<CosmosDbBuilder, CosmosDbContainer>.DisposeAsyncCore() ContainerLifetime<CosmosDbBuilder, CosmosDbContainer>.Container object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Extension Methods ResultExtensions.ToResult<T>(T?, Error) Remarks The database is created and reset through the application's own TDbContext resolved from the test host's service provider — so a context whose constructor takes more than its options works without extra plumbing. It is created once during host startup through InitializeAsync(IServiceProvider) and recreated after each test through ResetAsync(IServiceProvider). Only the emulator readiness probe and the best-effort scope teardown use a bare DbContext, because neither needs the model. Constructors CosmosTestContainerFixture(IMessageSink) Fixture that starts the Azure Cosmos DB Linux emulator in a container and registers TDbContext in the test host pointed at it. Connectivity goes through the container's own Testcontainers.CosmosDb.CosmosDbContainer.HttpClient, whose handler rewrites every request to the mapped emulator endpoint, so no certificate or endpoint plumbing is needed. When owned by a ContainerHost, every consuming factory works in its own emulator database, so parallel test classes never share data; data is reset between tests by recreating that database. Derived classes only supply the configuration key (and optionally pin the emulator image via Configure). protected CosmosTestContainerFixture(IMessageSink messageSink) Parameters messageSink IMessageSink The xUnit diagnostic message sink. It is created once during host startup through InitializeAsync(IServiceProvider) and recreated after each test through ResetAsync(IServiceProvider). Only the emulator readiness probe and the best-effort scope teardown use a bare DbContext, because neither needs the model. Properties ConnectionString Gets the connection string used to communicate with the containerized service. public override string ConnectionString { get; } Property Value string DatabaseName Gets the name of the emulator database used when this fixture is consumed without a scope; scopes append their scope identifier to it. Defaults to test-db. protected virtual string DatabaseName { get; } Property Value string Methods Configure() Configures the emulator container. The default uses the vnext emulator image; override to pin a specific image or tune the builder. protected override CosmosDbBuilder Configure() Returns CosmosDbBuilder The configured builder. ConfigureServices(IServiceCollection) Registers TDbContext in the test host, pointed at the emulator's default database. Invoked through the container extension point when this fixture is consumed without a scope. public override void ConfigureServices(IServiceCollection services) Parameters services IServiceCollection The service collection to configure. CreateScope(string) Creates a scope backed by its own database inside the shared emulator, named after scopeId. The scope's database is created during host startup, recreated between tests, and deleted (best-effort) when disposed; the emulator container keeps running for other scopes. public override ITestContainer CreateScope(string scopeId) Parameters scopeId string A short, unique, lowercase identifier for the scope; appended to DatabaseName. Returns ITestContainer The scoped container view. InitializeAsync() Called immediately after the class has been created, before it is used. protected override ValueTask InitializeAsync() Returns ValueTask InitializeAsync(IServiceProvider) Performs the resource's one-time startup setup. public ValueTask InitializeAsync(IServiceProvider serviceProvider) Parameters serviceProvider IServiceProvider The application's root service provider; create a scope from it to resolve scoped services. Returns ValueTask A task representing the asynchronous setup work. ResetAsync(IServiceProvider) Drops and recreates the default database after each test, giving every test a clean slate. The TDbContext is resolved from serviceProvider, so its model — and any constructor dependencies — come from the test host rather than being constructed here. public ValueTask ResetAsync(IServiceProvider serviceProvider) Parameters serviceProvider IServiceProvider The application's root service provider. Returns ValueTask A task representing the asynchronous reset work.

api/Vulthil.xUnit.Fixtures.ContainerHost.title: Class ContainerHost | Vulthil.SharedKernel
api/Vulthil.xUnit.Fixtures.ContainerHost.summary: Class ContainerHost Namespace Vulthil.xUnit.Fixtures Assembly Vulthil.xUnit.dll Assembly-level fixture that owns a set of shared test containers, so heavyweight infrastructure (database servers, message brokers, emulators) starts at most once per test run instead of once per test class. public abstract class ContainerHost : IAsyncLifetime, IAsyncDisposable Inheritance object ContainerHost Implements IAsyncLifetime IAsyncDisposable Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Extension Methods ResultExtensions.ToResult<T>(T?, Error) Remarks Register a derived host with [assembly: AssemblyFixture(typeof(MyContainerHost))] and accept it in the constructor of a BaseWebApplicationFactory<TEntryPoint>-derived class fixture. The factory consumes every container registered here through a per-factory scope view (see ITestContainerScopeProvider), so parallel test classes share the running containers without sharing state. Containers start lazily on first use and concurrent consumers share a single startup task, so a filtered test run only pays for the containers its factories actually consume. Assembly fixtures are used from many tests simultaneously; this host is safe for that concurrency. Constructors ContainerHost(IMessageSink) Assembly-level fixture that owns a set of shared test containers, so heavyweight infrastructure (database servers, message brokers, emulators) starts at most once per test run instead of once per test class. protected ContainerHost(IMessageSink messageSink) Parameters messageSink IMessageSink The xUnit diagnostic message sink, forwarded to container fixtures. Remarks Register a derived host with [assembly: AssemblyFixture(typeof(MyContainerHost))] and accept it in the constructor of a BaseWebApplicationFactory<TEntryPoint>-derived class fixture. The factory consumes every container registered here through a per-factory scope view (see ITestContainerScopeProvider), so parallel test classes share the running containers without sharing state. Containers start lazily on first use and concurrent consumers share a single startup task, so a filtered test run only pays for the containers its factories actually consume. Assembly fixtures are used from many tests simultaneously; this host is safe for that concurrency. Properties Containers Gets the containers registered on this host. public IReadOnlyCollection<ITestContainer> Containers { get; } Property Value IReadOnlyCollection<ITestContainer> MessageSink Gets the xUnit diagnostic message sink, for passing to container fixtures created by the host. protected IMessageSink MessageSink { get; } Property Value IMessageSink Methods AddContainer(ITestContainer) Registers a shared test container on this host. Call from the constructor or from ConfigureContainers(). protected void AddContainer(ITestContainer container) Parameters container ITestContainer The container to register. ConfigureContainers() Override to register shared containers by calling AddContainer(ITestContainer). protected virtual Task ConfigureContainers() Returns Task A task representing the asynchronous registration work. DisposeAsync() Disposes every container that was started. Invoked once by xUnit after the last test in the assembly. public ValueTask DisposeAsync() Returns ValueTask A task representing the asynchronous dispose work. EnsureStartedAsync(ITestContainer) Starts the given container if it has not been started yet. Thread-safe: concurrent callers share a single startup task, and a failed startup is observed by every consumer instead of being retried. public Task EnsureStartedAsync(ITestContainer container) Parameters container ITestContainer A container registered on this host. Returns Task A task that completes when the container is running. Exceptions InvalidOperationException The container is not registered on this host. InitializeAsync() Registers the configured containers. Invoked once by xUnit before the fixture is handed to its first consumer; containers themselves start lazily through EnsureStartedAsync(ITestContainer). public ValueTask InitializeAsync() Returns ValueTask A task representing the asynchronous registration work.

api/Vulthil.xUnit.Fixtures.ITestContaine.summary: Interface ITestContainerScopeProvider Namespace Vulthil.xUnit.Fixtures Assembly Vulthil.xUnit.dll Implemented by test containers that can mint per-scope views of themselves, so a single running container (typically owned by a ContainerHost assembly fixture) can serve many test classes in parallel without sharing state. A scope usually maps to an isolated namespace inside the shared container — a uniquely named database on a database server, a virtual host on a message broker, or a key prefix in a cache. public interface ITestContainerScopeProvider Extension Methods ResultExtensions.ToResult<T>(T?, Error) Methods CreateScope(string) Creates a view of this container that is isolated under scopeId. The returned view's Xunit.IAsyncLifetime.InitializeAsync() provisions the namespace (for example CREATE DATABASE) and its DisposeAsync() removes it again; the underlying container's own lifecycle is never affected by the view. ITestContainer CreateScope(string scopeId) Parameters scopeId string A short, unique, lowercase identifier for the scope, safe to embed in names. Returns ITestContainer The scoped container view.

Respawn-based data resets between tests. When owned by a ContainerHost, CreateScope(string) gives every consuming factory its own uniquely named database on the shared server, so parallel test classes never see each other's data. protected TestDatabaseContainerFixture(IMessageSink messageSink) Parameters messageSink IMessageSink Properties ConnectionStringKey Gets the configuration key name where the connection string should be injected. public abstract string ConnectionStringKey { get; } Property Value string DbAdapter Gets the Respawn database adapter matching the container's database engine. protected abstract IDbAdapter DbAdapter { get; } Property Value IDbAdapter Methods BuildScopedConnectionString(string) Builds a connection string pointing at databaseName on this server by rewriting the container's default connection string. Override when the provider does not use the Database keyword. protected virtual string BuildScopedConnectionString(string databaseName) Parameters databaseName string The database the returned connection string should target. Returns string The scoped connection string. ConfigureServices(IServiceCollection) Registers or replaces services for this container in the test host. Invoked through ConfigureTestServices, so it runs after the application's own registrations and can decorate or replace them (for example, repointing a DbContext at the containerized service). public virtual void ConfigureServices(IServiceCollection services) Parameters services IServiceCollection The service collection to configure. Remarks The base implementation decorates the application's DbContextOptions<TContext> registration to ignore ManyServiceProvidersCreatedWarning. With shared containers every test class fixture (and every per-test WithWebHostBuilder clone) builds its own host, and therefore its own EF Core internal service provider, so large suites trip that warning even though each host is correct. Overrides should call base to preserve this behavior. ConfigureWebHost(IWebHostBuilder) Configures the test host for this container. Invoked by BaseWebApplicationFactory<TEntryPoint> during host build, after connection strings are injected and before the factory's own ConfigureCustomWebHost. Use it to apply host settings the container needs. public virtual void ConfigureWebHost(IWebHostBuilder builder) Parameters builder IWebHostBuilder The web host builder to configure. CreateDatabaseAsync(string) Creates the database databaseName on the server, using a connection to the container's default database. Override for engines that need non-standard DDL. protected virtual Task CreateDatabaseAsync(string databaseName) Parameters databaseName string The name of the database to create. Returns Task A task representing the asynchronous work. CreateScope(string) Creates a scope view backed by its own database on this server, named after scopeId. The view creates the database when it initializes, applies migrations through MigrateDatabase(IServiceProvider), resets it with Respawn between tests, and drops it (best-effort) when disposed. The server container itself keeps running for other scopes. public virtual ITestContainer CreateScope(string scopeId) Parameters scopeId string A short, unique, lowercase identifier for the scope; sanitized into a database name. Returns ITestContainer The scoped container view. DropDatabaseAsync(string) Drops the database databaseName on the server. Invoked best-effort when a scope is disposed; failures are reported as diagnostics rather than test failures, because the server container is torn down with the host anyway. The default issues engine-appropriate DDL based on DbAdapter — PostgreSQL terminates lingering (pooled) sessions with WITH (FORCE), SQL Server switches the database to single-user first — so consumers normally never override this. protected virtual Task DropDatabaseAsync(string databaseName) Parameters databaseName string The name of the database to drop. Returns Task A task representing the asynchronous work. ExecuteServerCommandAsync(string) Executes a single DDL command against the container's default database. protected Task ExecuteServerCommandAsync(string commandText) Parameters commandText string The command text to execute. Returns Task A task representing the asynchronous work. MigrateDatabase(IServiceProvider) Ensures pending EF Core migrations are applied to the containerized database. Invoked during host startup, before the application's own background services run, so the schema exists in time; only migrations that remain pending are applied, and a concurrent migrator (such as an application that migrates itself) is tolerated rather than fought. public ValueTask MigrateDatabase(IServiceProvider serviceProvider) Parameters serviceProvider IServiceProvider The scoped service provider to resolve the DbContext from. Returns ValueTask A task representing the asynchronous migration work. ResetAsync(IServiceProvider) Resets the resource to a clean state after each test, with the application's root service provider so a resource that needs the app's services — for example a Cosmos store recreating its database through the registered DbContext — can resolve them from a fresh scope. Resources that need no services ignore the argument. One-time setup that must run before the first test instead belongs in IStartupResource. public ValueTask ResetAsync(IServiceProvider serviceProvider) Parameters serviceProvider IServiceProvider The application's root service provider; create a scope from it to resolve scoped services. Returns ValueTask A task representing the asynchronous reset work. SanitizeDatabaseName(string) Normalizes scopeId into an identifier that is safe to use unquoted as a database name on any supported engine: lowercase ASCII letters, digits and underscores, starting with a letter, at most 63 characters. protected static string SanitizeDatabaseName(string scopeId) Parameters scopeId string The scope identifier to normalize. Returns string The sanitized database name.

api/Vulthil.xUnit.Fixtures.html.title: Namespace Vulthil.xUnit.Fixtures | Vulthil.SharedKernel
api/Vulthil.xUnit.Fixtures.html.summary: Namespace Vulthil.xUnit.Fixtures Classes ContainerHost Assembly-level fixture that owns a set of shared test containers, so heavyweight infrastructure (database servers, message brokers, emulators) starts at most once per test run instead of once per test class. RabbitMqTestContainerFixture<TBuilderEntity, TContainerEntity> Fixture that wraps a RabbitMQ broker container. When owned by a ContainerHost, every consuming factory gets its own virtual host on the shared broker (created via rabbitmqctl inside the container and appended to the AMQP connection string), so parallel test classes never see each other's exchanges, queues or messages. Derived classes only configure the container and supply the connection string and key. TestContainerFixtureWithConnectionString<TBuilderEntity, TContainerEntity> Fixture that wraps a Testcontainers container and exposes a connection string for integration test configuration. TestContainerFixture<TBuilderEntity, TContainerEntity> Base fixture that wraps a Testcontainers container as an ITestContainer for use in BaseWebApplicationFactory<TEntryPoint>. TestDatabaseContainerFixture<TDbContext, TBuilderEntity, TContainerEntity> Fixture that wraps a database container, applies EF Core migrations, and supports Respawn-based data resets between tests. When owned by a ContainerHost, CreateScope(string) gives every consuming factory its own uniquely named database on the shared server, so parallel test classes never see each other's data. Interfaces ITestContainer Represents a test container that can be started and stopped as part of the test lifecycle, and that may participate in configuring the test host. ITestContainerScopeProvider Implemented by test containers that can mint per-scope views of themselves, so a single running container (typically owned by a ContainerHost assembly fixture) can serve many test classes in parallel without sharing state. A scope usually maps to an isolated namespace inside the shared container — a uniquely named database on a database server, a virtual host on a message broker, or a key prefix in a cache. ITestContainerWithConnectionString Extends ITestContainer with a connection string for use in integration tests. ITestDatabaseContainer Extends ITestContainerWithConnectionString with database migration capabilities. Data is reset between tests through ResetAsync(IServiceProvider) (Respawn).

Configure stubbed responses per test and inspect the requests the system under test sent. Register one per typed client via AddHttpMock<TClient, TImplementation>() on the factory and retrieve it with HttpMock<TClient>(). public interface IHttpMock : IResettableResource Inherited Members IResettableResource.ResetAsync(IServiceProvider) Extension Methods ResultExtensions.ToResult<T>(T?, Error) Properties ReceivedRequests Gets the requests received by this mock since the last reset, in the order they were sent. Returns IHttpMockResponseBuilder A builder for configuring the response. On(HttpMethod, string) Begins configuring a stubbed response for requests whose method and absolute path match. The pathPattern is matched against the request's absolute path and supports * wildcards. IHttpMockResponseBuilder On(HttpMethod method, string pathPattern) Parameters method HttpMethod The HTTP method to match. pathPattern string The absolute-path pattern to match, e.g. /users/*/repos. Returns IHttpMockResponseBuilder A builder for configuring the response.

api/Vulthil.xUnit.Http.IHttpMockResponse.title: Interface IHttpMockResponseBuilder | Vulthil.SharedKernel
api/Vulthil.xUnit.Http.IHttpMockResponse.summary: Interface IHttpMockResponseBuilder Namespace Vulthil.xUnit.Http Assembly Vulthil.xUnit.dll Configures the response a matched request should receive. Returned by the On methods on IHttpMock. Header methods apply to the response defined by the preceding RespondWith call and can be chained. public interface IHttpMockResponseBuilder Extension Methods ResultExtensions.ToResult<T>(T?, Error) Methods RespondWith(Func<HttpRequestMessage, CancellationToken, Task<HttpResponseMessage>>) Responds using a custom responder for full control over the HttpResponseMessage. The responder must return a fresh message on each invocation. IHttpMockResponseBuilder RespondWith(Func<HttpRequestMessage, CancellationToken, Task<HttpResponseMessage>> responder) Parameters responder Func<HttpRequestMessage, CancellationToken, Task<HttpResponseMessage>> The function that produces the response. Returns IHttpMockResponseBuilder The same builder for chaining. RespondWith(HttpStatusCode) Responds with the given status code and no body. IHttpMockResponseBuilder RespondWith(HttpStatusCode statusCode) Parameters statusCode HttpStatusCode The status code to return. Returns IHttpMockResponseBuilder The same builder for chaining. RespondWithJson(HttpStatusCode, string) Responds with the given status code and the supplied JSON string verbatim. Useful for replaying a real response captured from the live endpoint and stored as a JSON document. IHttpMockResponseBuilder RespondWithJson(HttpStatusCode statusCode, string json) Parameters statusCode HttpStatusCode The status code to return. json string The raw JSON to return as the response body. Returns IHttpMockResponseBuilder The same builder for chaining. RespondWith<TResponse>(HttpStatusCode, TResponse) Responds with the given status code and a JSON body produced by serializing body using web (camelCase) defaults. IHttpMockResponseBuilder RespondWith<TResponse>(HttpStatusCode statusCode, TResponse body) Parameters statusCode HttpStatusCode The status code to return. body TResponse The object to serialize as the JSON response body. Returns IHttpMockResponseBuilder The same builder for chaining. Type Parameters TResponse The response body type. WithHeader(string, params string[]) Adds a header to the response defined by the preceding RespondWith call. Content headers (such as Content-Type) are routed to the response content automatically. IHttpMockResponseBuilder WithHeader(string name, params string[] values) Parameters name string The header name. values string[] One or more header values. Returns IHttpMockResponseBuilder The same builder for chaining.

api/Vulthil.xUnit.IResettableResource.ht.title: Interface IResettableResource | Vulthil.SharedKernel
api/Vulthil.xUnit.IResettableResource.ht.summary: Interface IResettableResource Namespace Vulthil.xUnit Assembly Vulthil.xUnit.dll A test resource whose state can be reset to a clean baseline between tests — for example a database reset via Respawn, or an HTTP mock whose configured responses and captured requests are cleared. public interface IResettableResource Extension Methods ResultExtensions.ToResult<T>(T?, Error) Remarks BaseIntegrationTestCase<TFactory, TEntryPoint> resets every registered resettable resource after each test. Methods ResetAsync(IServiceProvider) Resets the resource to a clean state after each test, with the application's root service provider so a resource that needs the app's services — for example a Cosmos store recreating its database through the registered DbContext — can resolve them from a fresh scope. Resources that need no services ignore the argument. One-time setup that must run before the first test instead belongs in IStartupResource. ValueTask ResetAsync(IServiceProvider serviceProvider) Parameters serviceProvider IServiceProvider The application's root service provider; create a scope from it to resolve scoped services. Returns ValueTask A task representing the asynchronous reset work.

api/Vulthil.xUnit.IStartupResource.html.title: Interface IStartupResource | Vulthil.SharedKernel
api/Vulthil.xUnit.IStartupResource.html.summary: Interface IStartupResource Namespace Vulthil.xUnit Assembly Vulthil.xUnit.dll A test resource that performs one-time setup during host startup using the application's services — for example a Cosmos store creating its database and containers through the registered DbContext. Invoked once, after database migrations and before the application's background services, so the resource is ready before the first test runs. public interface IStartupResource Extension Methods ResultExtensions.ToResult<T>(T?, Error) Remarks Distinct from IResettableResource: startup setup runs once before any test, whereas a reset runs after each test. A resource may implement both — creating its store at startup and recreating it between tests. Methods InitializeAsync(IServiceProvider) Performs the resource's one-time startup setup. ValueTask InitializeAsync(IServiceProvider serviceProvider) Parameters serviceProvider IServiceProvider The application's root service provider; create a scope from it to resolve scoped services. Returns ValueTask A task representing the asynchronous setup work.

api/Vulthil.xUnit.html.title: Namespace Vulthil.xUnit | Vulthil.SharedKernel
api/Vulthil.xUnit.html.summary: Namespace Vulthil.xUnit Classes BaseIntegrationTestCase<TFactory, TEntryPoint> Base class for integration tests that use a BaseWebApplicationFactory<TEntryPoint> with container-based infrastructure. BaseUnitTestCase Base class for unit tests providing auto-mocking, lifecycle hooks, and a shared cancellation token. BaseUnitTestCase<TTarget> Base class for unit tests that automatically creates and exposes a lazily-initialized target instance of TTarget. BaseWebApplicationFactory<TEntryPoint> Abstract WebApplicationFactory<TEntryPoint> that manages a set of test containers, injects their connection strings into the test host, and ensures EF Core migrations are applied during host startup. Interfaces IResettableResource A test resource whose state can be reset to a clean baseline between tests — for example a database reset via Respawn, or an HTTP mock whose configured responses and captured requests are cleared. IStartupResource A test resource that performs one-time setup during host startup using the application's services — for example a Cosmos store creating its database and containers through the registered DbContext. Invoked once, after database migrations and before the application's background services, so the resource is ready before the first test runs.

articles/cqrs-pipeline.html.title: CQRS & Pipeline Behaviors | Vulthil.SharedKernel
articles/cqrs-pipeline.html.summary: CQRS & Pipeline Behaviors Vulthil.SharedKernel.Application implements the CQRS (Command Query Responsibility Segregation) pattern with a lightweight in-process sender and configurable pipeline behaviors. Concepts Abstraction Description ICommand / ICommand<TResponse> Write operation that changes state ITransactionalCommand / ITransactionalCommand<TResponse> Command executed inside a database transaction IQuery<TResponse> Read operation that returns data ICommandHandler<TCommand, TResponse> Handles a command IQueryHandler<TQuery, TResponse> Handles a query ISender Dispatches requests to their handlers IPipelineHandler<TRequest, TResponse> Cross-cutting middleware for the handler pipeline Registration builder.Services.AddApplication(options => { // Scan assemblies for handlers options.RegisterHandlerAssemblies(typeof(Program).Assembly); // Scan assemblies for FluentValidation validators options.RegisterFluentValidationAssemblies(typeof(Program).Assembly); // Add pipeline behaviors (order matters) options.AddValidationPipelineBehavior(); options.AddRequestLoggingBehavior(); options.AddTransactionalPipelineBehavior(); }); Defining Commands and Handlers public sealed record CreateUserCommand(string Email) : ICommand<Result<Guid>>; public sealed class CreateUserCommandHandler(IUserRepository users, IUnitOfWork uow) : ICommandHandler<CreateUserCommand, Result<Guid>> { public async Task<Result<Guid>> HandleAsync( CreateUserCommand request, CancellationToken cancellationToken) { var user = User.Create(request.Email); users.Add(user); await uow.SaveChangesAsync(cancellationToken); return user.Id.Value; } } Defining Queries and Handlers public sealed record GetUserQuery(Guid UserId) : IQuery<Result<UserDto>>; public sealed class GetUserQueryHandler(AppDbContext db) : IQueryHandler<GetUserQuery, Result<UserDto>> { public async Task<Result<UserDto>> HandleAsync( GetUserQuery request, CancellationToken cancellationToken) { var user = await db.Users .Where(u => u.Id == new UserId(request.UserId)) .Select(u => new UserDto(u.Id.Value, u.Email)) .FirstOrDefaultAsync(cancellationToken); return user is not null ? Result.Success(user) : Result.Failure<UserDto>(UserErrors.NotFound); } } Pipeline Behaviors Pipeline behaviors wrap every handler invocation, allowing you to add cross-cutting logic without modifying individual handlers. Validation ValidationPipelineBehavior runs all registered IValidator<TCommand> instances before the handler executes. When validation fails it short-circuits: a command returning Result or Result<T> receives a failed result containing a ValidationError, while a command with any other response type throws a ValidationException (there is no in-band way to represent failure for a non-result response): public sealed class CreateUserCommandValidator : AbstractValidator<CreateUserCommand> { public CreateUserCommandValidator() { RuleFor(x => x.Email) .NotEmpty() .EmailAddress(); } } No additional wiring is needed – the validator is picked up automatically from the registered assemblies. Request Logging RequestLoggingPipelineBehavior logs the start and completion of every request, attaching the serialised error to the logging scope when the result is a failure. Transactional TransactionalPipelineBehavior wraps ITransactionalCommand handlers inside a database transaction. If the handler returns a successful Result, the transaction commits; if it returns a failed Result or throws, the transaction rolls back. (The non-generic ITransactionalCommand marker returns Result and is wrapped the same way as ITransactionalCommand<Result>.) // Mark a command as transactional public sealed record TransferFundsCommand(Guid FromAccount, Guid ToAccount, decimal Amount) : ITransactionalCommand<Result>; Dispatching Requests Inject ISender into your API endpoints or controllers: app.MapPost("/users", async (CreateUserCommand command, ISender sender) => { var result = await sender.SendAsync(command); return result.ToIResult(); }); Direct handler injection You can also inject the handler interface directly. The resolved instance is the same pipeline-wrapped handler that ISender would dispatch to, so behaviors (validation, logging, transactions, custom ones) apply uniformly either way. app.MapPost("/users", async ( CreateUserCommand command, ICommandHandler<CreateUserCommand, Result<Guid>> handler) => { var result = await handler.HandleAsync(command); return result.ToIResult(); }); The interfaces you can inject are IHandler<TRequest, TResponse>, ICommandHandler<TCommand, TResponse>, ICommandHandler<TCommand> (for Result-returning commands) and IQueryHandler<TQuery, TResponse>. The concrete handler implementation is not registered as a DI service — there is no way to bypass the pipeline by injecting the concrete type. Behaviors across assemblies Pipeline behaviors are composed at handler-resolution time, not at registration time, so the order of registration is irrelevant. Different assemblies may register handlers and behaviors independently — all behaviors registered before BuildServiceProvider apply to all handlers resolved afterwards. // In an Infrastructure assembly: services.AddOpenPipelineHandler(typeof(MyCustomBehavior<,>));

articles/domain-modeling.html.title: Domain Modeling | Vulthil.SharedKernel
articles/domain-modeling.html.summary: Domain Modeling Vulthil.SharedKernel provides base types for building a rich domain model following Domain-Driven Design conventions. Primitives Type Description Entity<TId> Base class for entities identified by a strongly-typed ID AggregateRoot<TId> Entity subclass that tracks and raises domain events IDomainEvent Marker interface for domain events IDomainEventHandler<T> Handler contract for processing domain events DomainException Base exception for domain invariant violations Defining an Entity Entities are compared by identity, not by attribute values. Give the identifier value equality — a record or readonly record struct — so two entities with the same id compare as equal: public sealed record UserId(Guid Value); public sealed class User : AggregateRoot<UserId> { public string Email { get; private set; } private User(UserId id, string email) : base(id) { Email = email; } public static User Create(string email) { var user = new User(new UserId(Guid.NewGuid()), email); user.Raise(new UserCreatedEvent(user.Id)); return user; } public void ChangeEmail(string newEmail) { Email = newEmail; Raise(new UserEmailChangedEvent(Id, newEmail)); } } Key points: The constructor is private – creation goes through a factory method that enforces invariants.
